message.content: I want to build websearch capabilities for minima harness. I want you to build me mini projects that needs to be done step by step to build this feature. I am a junior swe, who wants to do learn fundamentals and complete these mini projects to build this feature for Minima harness. Create MD file guide for me, and ask me questions to get more info from me if needed

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## codaph
Codaph MCP exposes local Codaph project registry, status, sessions, timeline, and diff summaries. Pass `cwd` or `project_path` to target a repo.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.text: Perfect. Let me explore the main modules and read the key documentation:

content.content:
1	# Minima
2	
3	**Recommend a cheaper LLM model for each task, so LLM-driven workflows spend fewer tokens
4	without losing the quality the task actually needs.**
5	
6	Minima **only recommends** — it never proxies a call, runs a model, rewrites a prompt, or
7	caches. It is a stack-agnostic advice layer backed by [Mubit](https://docs.mubit.ai) memory:
8	ask which model to use, run that model yourself, then tell Minima how it went. Because it
9	sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, **it adds zero latency to your real LLM
10	request.**
11	
12	```
13	  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
14	   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce memory)
15	        ▲                                                           │
16	        └──────────────  recommendations get sharper  ─────────────┘
17	```
18	
19	## Why it works
20	
21	Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) memory. Every `POST /v1/feedback` writes a `task → model → outcome` record; every `POST /v1/recommend` recalls the most similar past records and picks the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar. The longer it runs, the sharper the picks.
22	
23	A `cost_quality_tradeoff` slider (0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality) moves the
24	bar. When memory is thin or conflicting, Minima can escalate to a cheap-LLM reasoner
25	(configurable, off by default).
26	
27	### Cost ranking that reflects reality
28	
29	A flat token estimate assumes a fixed completion length, so it ignores reasoning/thinking
30	tokens and mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima
31	ranks candidates by what they **really** cost, choosing one basis for the whole candidate
32	set:
33	
34	- **rescaled** (best) — this request's input priced + the model's *observed* output-token
35	  behavior; size-exact **and** reasoning-aware.
36	- **observed** — robust median of realized `$/call` from recalled outcomes.
37	- **estimate** (cold start) — token estimate from catalog prices.
38	
39	The basis climbs `estimate → observed → rescaled` as your `/feedback` calls accumulate
40	realized tokens and cost. See [Concepts → Cost-basis tiers](docs/concepts.md#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled).
41	
42	## Endpoints
43	
44	| Endpoint | Purpose |
45	|----------|---------|
46	| `POST /v1/recommend` | Recommend a model for one task. |
47	| `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` | Recommend a model per step of a multi-step workflow. |
48	| `POST /v1/feedback` | Report an outcome and close the learning loop. |
49	| `GET  /v1/models` | The current model catalog (cost + capability priors). |
50	| `GET  /v1/strategies` | Rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace (explainability). |
51	| `GET  /v1/health` | Service, Mubit, catalog, and reasoner status. |
52	| `POST\|GET\|DELETE /v1/admin/tenants` | Tenant provisioning (multi-tenant mode only). |
53	
54	Full schemas, fields, warnings, and error formats: **[API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)**.
55	
56	## Quickstart
57	
58	```bash
59	uv sync --extra dev
60	cp .env.example .env                       # set MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MUBIT_ENDPOINT if not local)
61	
62	# optional: seed cold-start memory so day-one picks are grounded
63	uv run minima-seed --dataset synthetic --limit 2000 --lane minima:default
64	
65	make run                                   # uvicorn on :8080 (interactive docs at /docs)
66	```
67	
68	```bash
69	# recommend
70	curl -s localhost:8080/v1/recommend -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
71	  "task": {"task": "Summarize this incident report into 3 bullets.",
72	           "task_type": "summarization"},
73	  "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3
74	}' | jq
75	
76	# ...run the recommended model yourself, then close the loop
77	curl -s localhost:8080/v1/feedback -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
78	  "recommendation_id": "<from above>", "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
79	  "outcome": "success", "quality_score": 0.95,
80	  "input_tokens": 1760, "output_tokens": 110, "actual_cost_usd": 0.0021,
81	  "verified_in_production": true
82	}' | jq
83	```
84	
85	Minima talks to a Mubit runtime at `MUBIT_ENDPOINT` (defaults to `http://127.0.0.1:3000`;
86	start one with `make run-mubit` in the Mubit repo) and uses Mubit's server-side embeddings,
87	so it needs no embedding model of its own.
88	
89	## Python client
90	
91	```python
92	from minima_client import MinimaClient
93	
94	with MinimaClient("http://localhost:8080") as minima:
95	    rec = minima.recommend("Write a Python CSV parser.", cost_quality_tradeoff=3)
96	    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id yourself ...
97	    minima.feedback(rec.recommendation_id, rec.recommended_model.model_id, "success",
98	                    quality_score=0.95, input_tokens=180, output_tokens=640,
99	                    actual_cost_usd=0.0034, verified_in_production=True)
100	```
101	
102	Sync + async clients and zero-code `autocapture`: **[Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md)**.
103	
104	## Documentation
105	
106	| Doc | What's in it |
107	|-----|--------------|
108	| [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md) | Install, configure, run, first recommendation. |
109	| [Concepts](docs/concepts.md) | The loop, the algorithm, cost-basis tiers, escalation, how it improves. |
110	| [API Reference](docs/api-reference.md) | Every endpoint, full schemas, warnings, errors. |
111	| [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) | Every environment variable + tuning guidance. |
112	| [Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md) | `minima_client` clients + autocapture. |
113	| [Cold-Start Seeding](docs/seeding.md) | Load history so day-one picks are grounded. |
114	| [Multi-Tenancy](docs/multi-tenancy.md) | One deployment, many orgs, per-org Mubit instances. |
115	| [Operations](docs/operations.md) | Deployment, health, degradation, monitoring, secrets. |
116	| [Examples](docs/examples.md) | Guided tour of the runnable examples. |
117	| [Agent Harness](docs/harness.md) | `minima_harness`: a Minima-routing port of PI's agent toolkit. |
118	
119	## Examples
120	
121	Runnable, progressively advanced — in **[`examples/`](examples/)**:
122	
123	| # | Example | Shows |
124	|---|---------|-------|
125	| 1 | [`01_quickstart.sh`](examples/01_quickstart.sh) | Raw `curl` against every endpoint. |
126	| 2 | [`02_recommend_and_feedback.py`](examples/02_recommend_and_feedback.py) | The core loop with the SDK. |
127	| 3 | [`03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py`](examples/03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py) | Constraints + slider sweep. |
128	| 4 | [`04_workflow.py`](examples/04_workflow.py) | Per-step workflow recommendations. |
129	| 5 | [`05_autocapture.py`](examples/05_autocapture.py) | Zero-code intake via `mubit.learn`. |
130	| 6 | [`06_routed_llm_call.py`](examples/06_routed_llm_call.py) | Routing a real Claude call + feedback. |
131	| 7 | [`07_multitenant_admin.py`](examples/07_multitenant_admin.py) | Provision an org, call as that tenant. |
132	| 8 | [`harness_warmup.py`](examples/harness_warmup.py) | The `minima_harness` agent loop (demo mode needs no keys). |
133	
134	## Agent harness
135	
136	[`minima_harness/`](src/minima_harness) is a lean Python port of
137	[`@earendil-works/pi`](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi)'s agent toolkit, made
138	Minima-native: an `Agent` runtime with tool calling **plus** a `MinimaAgent` that routes
139	every prompt through Minima and feeds the realized tokens/cost/quality back. It is the
140	"run the model yourself" half of the Minima loop, packaged.
141	
142	```python
143	from minima_harness.minima import MinimaAgent, HarnessConfig
144	
145	agent = MinimaAgent(HarnessConfig.from_env())   # MINIMA_URL, candidates, judge policy
146	await agent.prompt("Summarize this incident.", task_type="summarization", slider=3)
147	# -> Minima picked the model, the agent ran it, judged quality, fed the outcome back
148	```
149	
150	Try it with no keys via the in-process demo:
151	
152	```bash
153	uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py          # demo (in-process Minima + fake provider)
154	uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live   # real Minima + real providers
155	```
156	
157	Full architecture, the loop mapping, and extension guide:
158	**[Agent Harness](docs/harness.md)**.
159	
160	## Configuration
161	
162	All configuration is via environment variables (see [`.env.example`](.env.example) and
163	[Configuration](docs/configuration.md)). The only required value is `MUBIT_API_KEY` (in
164	single-tenant mode). Notable knobs:
165	
166	- `MINIMA_USE_OBSERVED_COST` / `MINIMA_OBSERVED_COST_MIN_N` — rank by realized cost.
167	- `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER` — enable the cheap-LLM escalation tier (`anthropic` / `gemini`).
168	- `MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=sqlite` — durable recommendation resolution (multi-worker).
169	- `MINIMA_MULTITENANT` — serve many orgs from one deployment.
170	
171	## Development
172	
173	```bash
174	make install     # uv sync --extra dev
175	make test        # unit + integration (no Mubit needed)
176	make lint        # ruff + mypy
177	make live        # end-to-end against a running Mubit (pytest -m live)
178	make eval        # offline RouterBench savings evaluation (pytest -m eval)
179	make fmt         # ruff --fix + format
180	make seed        # minima-seed (LIMIT=, LANE= overridable)
181	```
182	
183	## Project layout
184

186	src/minima/
187	  api/routers/      recommend · feedback · models · strategies · health · admin
188	  recommender/      engine · classify · aggregate · score · escalation · propensity · recstore
189	  memory/           adapter (only Mubit touchpoint) · records · keys · threadpool
190	  catalog/          store · merge · refresh · sources/{litellm,openrouter} · data/*.json
191	  llm/              base · anthropic · gemini · registry   (the escalation reasoner)
192	  tenancy/          runtime · registry · context · keys · secrets
193	  seeding/          routerbench · synthetic · run_seed (minima-seed CLI)
194	  schemas/          common · recommend · workflow · feedback · models_catalog · strategies · admin
195	src/minima_harness/   ported pi-ai (ai/) + pi-agent-core (agent/) + Minima integration (minima/) — see docs/harness.md
196	client_sdk/minima_client/   client (sync+async) · autocapture · errors
197	docs/               full documentation       examples/   runnable examples
198	tests/              unit · integration (FakeMemory) · live (-m live) · eval (-m eval)
199	```
200	
201	## License

file.content:
# Minima

**Recommend a cheaper LLM model for each task, so LLM-driven workflows spend fewer tokens
without losing the quality the task actually needs.**

Minima **only recommends** — it never proxies a call, runs a model, rewrites a prompt, or
caches. It is a stack-agnostic advice layer backed by [Mubit](https://docs.mubit.ai) memory:
ask which model to use, run that model yourself, then tell Minima how it went. Because it
sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, **it adds zero latency to your real LLM
request.**

```
  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce memory)
        ▲                                                           │
        └──────────────  recommendations get sharper  ─────────────┘
```

## Why it works

Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) memory. Every `POST /v1/feedback` writes a `task → model → outcome` record; every `POST /v1/recommend` recalls the most similar past records and picks the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar. The longer it runs, the sharper the picks.

A `cost_quality_tradeoff` slider (0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality) moves the
bar. When memory is thin or conflicting, Minima can escalate to a cheap-LLM reasoner
(configurable, off by default).

### Cost ranking that reflects reality

A flat token estimate assumes a fixed completion length, so it ignores reasoning/thinking
tokens and mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima
ranks candidates by what they **really** cost, choosing one basis for the whole candidate
set:

- **rescaled** (best) — this request's input priced + the model's *observed* output-token
  behavior; size-exact **and** reasoning-aware.
- **observed** — robust median of realized `$/call` from recalled outcomes.
- **estimate** (cold start) — token estimate from catalog prices.

The basis climbs `estimate → observed → rescaled` as your `/feedback` calls accumulate
realized tokens and cost. See [Concepts → Cost-basis tiers](docs/concepts.md#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled).

## Endpoints

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `POST /v1/recommend` | Recommend a model for one task. |
| `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` | Recommend a model per step of a multi-step workflow. |
| `POST /v1/feedback` | Report an outcome and close the learning loop. |
| `GET  /v1/models` | The current model catalog (cost + capability priors). |
| `GET  /v1/strategies` | Rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace (explainability). |
| `GET  /v1/health` | Service, Mubit, catalog, and reasoner status. |
| `POST\|GET\|DELETE /v1/admin/tenants` | Tenant provisioning (multi-tenant mode only). |

Full schemas, fields, warnings, and error formats: **[API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)**.

## Quickstart

```bash
uv sync --extra dev
cp .env.example .env                       # set MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MUBIT_ENDPOINT if not local)

# optional: seed cold-start memory so day-one picks are grounded
uv run minima-seed --dataset synthetic --limit 2000 --lane minima:default

make run                                   # uvicorn on :8080 (interactive docs at /docs)
```

```bash
# recommend
curl -s localhost:8080/v1/recommend -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
  "task": {"task": "Summarize this incident report into 3 bullets.",
           "task_type": "summarization"},
  "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3
}' | jq

# ...run the recommended model yourself, then close the loop
curl -s localhost:8080/v1/feedback -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
  "recommendation_id": "<from above>", "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
  "outcome": "success", "quality_score": 0.95,
  "input_tokens": 1760, "output_tokens": 110, "actual_cost_usd": 0.0021,
  "verified_in_production": true
}' | jq
```

Minima talks to a Mubit runtime at `MUBIT_ENDPOINT` (defaults to `http://127.0.0.1:3000`;
start one with `make run-mubit` in the Mubit repo) and uses Mubit's server-side embeddings,
so it needs no embedding model of its own.

## Python client

```python
from minima_client import MinimaClient

with MinimaClient("http://localhost:8080") as minima:
    rec = minima.recommend("Write a Python CSV parser.", cost_quality_tradeoff=3)
    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id yourself ...
    minima.feedback(rec.recommendation_id, rec.recommended_model.model_id, "success",
                    quality_score=0.95, input_tokens=180, output_tokens=640,
                    actual_cost_usd=0.0034, verified_in_production=True)
```

Sync + async clients and zero-code `autocapture`: **[Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md)**.

## Documentation

| Doc | What's in it |
|-----|--------------|
| [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md) | Install, configure, run, first recommendation. |
| [Concepts](docs/concepts.md) | The loop, the algorithm, cost-basis tiers, escalation, how it improves. |
| [API Reference](docs/api-reference.md) | Every endpoint, full schemas, warnings, errors. |
| [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) | Every environment variable + tuning guidance. |
| [Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md) | `minima_client` clients + autocapture. |
| [Cold-Start Seeding](docs/seeding.md) | Load history so day-one picks are grounded. |
| [Multi-Tenancy](docs/multi-tenancy.md) | One deployment, many orgs, per-org Mubit instances. |
| [Operations](docs/operations.md) | Deployment, health, degradation, monitoring, secrets. |
| [Examples](docs/examples.md) | Guided tour of the runnable examples. |
| [Agent Harness](docs/harness.md) | `minima_harness`: a Minima-routing port of PI's agent toolkit. |

## Examples

Runnable, progressively advanced — in **[`examples/`](examples/)**:

| # | Example | Shows |
|---|---------|-------|
| 1 | [`01_quickstart.sh`](examples/01_quickstart.sh) | Raw `curl` against every endpoint. |
| 2 | [`02_recommend_and_feedback.py`](examples/02_recommend_and_feedback.py) | The core loop with the SDK. |
| 3 | [`03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py`](examples/03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py) | Constraints + slider sweep. |
| 4 | [`04_workflow.py`](examples/04_workflow.py) | Per-step workflow recommendations. |
| 5 | [`05_autocapture.py`](examples/05_autocapture.py) | Zero-code intake via `mubit.learn`. |
| 6 | [`06_routed_llm_call.py`](examples/06_routed_llm_call.py) | Routing a real Claude call + feedback. |
| 7 | [`07_multitenant_admin.py`](examples/07_multitenant_admin.py) | Provision an org, call as that tenant. |
| 8 | [`harness_warmup.py`](examples/harness_warmup.py) | The `minima_harness` agent loop (demo mode needs no keys). |

## Agent harness

[`minima_harness/`](src/minima_harness) is a lean Python port of
[`@earendil-works/pi`](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi)'s agent toolkit, made
Minima-native: an `Agent` runtime with tool calling **plus** a `MinimaAgent` that routes
every prompt through Minima and feeds the realized tokens/cost/quality back. It is the
"run the model yourself" half of the Minima loop, packaged.

```python
from minima_harness.minima import MinimaAgent, HarnessConfig

agent = MinimaAgent(HarnessConfig.from_env())   # MINIMA_URL, candidates, judge policy
await agent.prompt("Summarize this incident.", task_type="summarization", slider=3)
# -> Minima picked the model, the agent ran it, judged quality, fed the outcome back
```

Try it with no keys via the in-process demo:

```bash
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py          # demo (in-process Minima + fake provider)
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live   # real Minima + real providers
```

Full architecture, the loop mapping, and extension guide:
**[Agent Harness](docs/harness.md)**.

## Configuration

All configuration is via environment variables (see [`.env.example`](.env.example) and
[Configuration](docs/configuration.md)). The only required value is `MUBIT_API_KEY` (in
single-tenant mode). Notable knobs:

- `MINIMA_USE_OBSERVED_COST` / `MINIMA_OBSERVED_COST_MIN_N` — rank by realized cost.
- `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER` — enable the cheap-LLM escalation tier (`anthropic` / `gemini`).
- `MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=sqlite` — durable recommendation resolution (multi-worker).
- `MINIMA_MULTITENANT` — serve many orgs from one deployment.

## Development

```bash
make install     # uv sync --extra dev
make test        # unit + integration (no Mubit needed)
make lint        # ruff + mypy
make live        # end-to-end against a running Mubit (pytest -m live)
make eval        # offline RouterBench savings evaluation (pytest -m eval)
make fmt         # ruff --fix + format
make seed        # minima-seed (LIMIT=, LANE= overridable)
```

## Project layout

```
src/minima/
  api/routers/      recommend · feedback · models · strategies · health · admin
  recommender/      engine · classify · aggregate · score · escalation · propensity · recstore
  memory/           adapter (only Mubit touchpoint) · records · keys · threadpool
  catalog/          store · merge · refresh · sources/{litellm,openrouter} · data/*.json
  llm/              base · anthropic · gemini · registry   (the escalation reasoner)
  tenancy/          runtime · registry · context · keys · secrets
  seeding/          routerbench · synthetic · run_seed (minima-seed CLI)
  schemas/          common · recommend · workflow · feedback · models_catalog · strategies · admin
src/minima_harness/   ported pi-ai (ai/) + pi-agent-core (agent/) + Minima integration (minima/) — see docs/harness.md
client_sdk/minima_client/   client (sync+async) · autocapture · errors
docs/               full documentation       examples/   runnable examples
tests/              unit · integration (FakeMemory) · live (-m live) · eval (-m eval)
```

## License

Minima is **source-available** under the [Functional Source License,
Version 1.1, Apache 2.0 Future License](LICENSE) (`FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0`).

You may use, copy, modify, and self-host Minima for any **Permitted Purpose** —
internal use, non-commercial education/research, and professional services for a
licensee. The one restriction is a **Competing Use**: you may not offer Minima
(or a substantially similar product/service) to others as a commercial or hosted
offering that competes with us. Two years after each version is published, that
version automatically converts to the **Apache License 2.0**.

Copyright 2026 Mubit.

content.content:
1	# Agent Harness (`minima_harness`)
2	
3	A lean Python port of [`@earendil-works/pi`](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi)'s
4	agent toolkit (`pi-ai` + `pi-agent-core`), made **Minima-native**: every prompt is routed
5	through Minima, and every realized outcome (tokens / cost / latency / quality) is fed
6	back so Minima's memory sharpens. It lives alongside the Minima service in this repo at
7	[`src/minima_harness/`](../src/minima_harness/) and ships in the same wheel.
8	
9	> The port is a from-scratch reimplementation of PI's design and APIs (no source copied);
10	> see `src/minima_harness/LICENSE_PI` for MIT attribution. The Minima integration layer is
11	> original work.
12	
13	## The loop
14	
15	```
16	MinimaAgent.prompt(task)
17	   │
18	   ├─ 1. ROUTE   ── MinimaRouter.recommend() ──▶ POST /v1/recommend
19	   │                sets agent.state.model (mapped from Minima's RankedModel)
20	   │
21	   ├─ 2. RUN     ── Agent loop (ported pi-agent-core)
22	   │                streams the model, executes tools, may run several turns
23	   │
24	   └─ 3. FEED BACK ─ MinimaRouter.feedback() ──▶ POST /v1/feedback
25	                     realized input_tokens / output_tokens / actual_cost_usd /
26	                     latency_ms + judged quality  ➜ outcome ∈ {success, partial, failure}
27	```
28	
29	Minima never runs the model — the harness owns model dispatch (via the ported `ai`
30	provider layer) and quality judging. Routing is **bypassable**: if Minima is unreachable
31	and `allow_offline=True`, the run proceeds on the current model with no feedback.
32	
33	## Architecture
34	
35	```
36	src/minima_harness/
37	├── ai/                 ported pi-ai: unified multi-provider LLM API
38	│   ├── types.py          Model · Context · Message · ContentBlock · AssistantMessage · Usage
39	│   ├── registry.py       get_model/get_models/get_providers + seed catalog
40	│   ├── stream.py         stream() / complete()  (+ Stream wrapper with .result())
41	│   ├── tools.py          Tool + validate_tool_call  (pydantic params)
42	│   ├── usage.py          realized cost = tokens × model.price
43	│   ├── compat.py         cross-provider: foreign thinking → <thinking> text tags
44	│   └── providers/        base Protocol + faux (tests) · anthropic · google · openai_compat(httpx)
45	├── agent/              ported pi-agent-core: stateful agent runtime
46	│   ├── events.py         agent_start/end · turn_* · message_* · tool_execution_*
47	│   ├── tools.py          AgentTool · ToolResult · before/afterToolCall hooks
48	│   ├── state.py          AgentState + AgentLoopConfig
49	│   ├── loop.py           agent_loop async generator + tool execution (parallel via anyio)
50	│   └── agent.py          Agent: prompt/continue_/abort/wait_for_idle/subscribe/steer/follow_up
51	├── minima/             the Minima-native layer (original work)
52	│   ├── config.py         HarnessConfig (MINIMA_URL, candidates, namespace, judge policy)
53	│   ├── mapping.py        RankedModel → harness Model (tolerant: exact → id → provider/model)
54	│   ├── judge.py          QualityJudge Protocol · LLMJudge · DeterministicJudge · ConstJudge
55	│   ├── router.py         MinimaRouter: recommend → RoutingResult, feedback (realized cost)
56	│   └── runtime.py        MinimaAgent(Agent): route → run → judge → feedback per prompt
57	└── tasks/              task corpus + grade_outcome thresholds
58	    └── task_set.py        Task dataclass + sample tasks (absorbed from agent/task_set.py)
59	```
60	
61	## Quickstart
62	
63	### Install
64	
65	```bash
66	pip install "minima-cli[harness,tui]"      # or: uv tool install "minima-cli[harness,tui]"
67	brew install mubit-ai/minima/minima         # Homebrew (see packaging/homebrew/README.md)
68	```
69	
70	The command is **`minima`** (`minima-harness` stays as an alias). Then run `minima config`
71	to add your keys (see below).
72	
73	### Demo mode (no keys, no services)
74	
75	```bash
76	uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py
77	```
78	
79	Spins an in-process Minima (`FakeMemory`) + a fake Anthropic provider and runs the task
80	corpus through `MinimaAgent` end to end. Prints the per-task routing decision, quality,
81	outcome, realized tokens/cost, and how many outcomes landed in memory.
82	
83	### Configure credentials (`minima config`)
84	
85	For an installed CLI, store keys **per-user** instead of a per-directory `.env`:
86	
87	```bash
88	minima config            # guided setup, two sections: LLM keys + Mubit/Minima
89	minima config list       # masked view of what's set + where (keyring | file)
90	minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY <key>
91	minima config doctor     # presence check + authenticated Minima probe (no secrets printed)
92	```
93	
94	Secrets go to the **OS keyring** (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / libsecret)
95	when available, falling back to `~/.minima-harness/config.env` at mode `0600`. Non-secret
96	config (`MINIMA_URL`) always lives in the file. At launch the store is loaded into the
97	environment with **lowest precedence** — a shell `export`, a project `./.env.harness`, or
98	`./.env` still override it. The same surface is available in-TUI via `/config`.
99	
100	Sections: **LLM provider keys** (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) ·
101	**Mubit / Minima routing** (`MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MINIMA_URL`, optional `MINIMA_API_KEY` →
102	falls back to `MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MUBIT_ENDPOINT`).
103	
104	### Live mode (real Minima + real providers)
105	
106	```bash
107	make run                       # Minima on :8080 in another terminal
108	export MUBIT_API_KEY=...       # passthrough auth on Minima
109	export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...   # for the candidate models you allow
110	uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live --rounds 3
111	```
112	
113	### As a library
114	
115	```python
116	import asyncio
117	from minima_harness.minima import HarnessConfig, MinimaAgent
118	from minima_harness.minima.judge import DeterministicJudge
119	
120	async def main():
121	    agent = MinimaAgent(HarnessConfig.from_env())   # MINIMA_URL, MINIMA_API_KEY, candidates…
122	    # Optional: judge each output with your own scorer instead of the default LLM grader.
123	    agent.judge = DeterministicJudge(lambda output: 0.9 if "done" in output else 0.2)
124	    routing = await agent.prompt("Refactor foo() to be async.", task_type="code", slider=7)
125	    print(routing.chosen_model_id, routing.decision_basis)
126	
127	asyncio.run(main())
128	```
129	
130	## The integration points
131	
132	**Routing granularity.** One recommend + one feedback per `prompt()`. A multi-turn
133	tool run uses the routed model throughout; each top-level prompt is one Minima outcome.
134	Call `prompt()` repeatedly in a conversation and every turn is routed and fed back
135	independently.
136	
137	**Realized cost, not Minima's prior.** Feedback sends `actual_cost_usd = usage.cost.total`
138	computed from the provider's *actual* token counts × the harness registry prices. This is
139	what lets Minima climb `estimate → observed → rescaled` — its single biggest accuracy
140	lever. Do not echo Minima's `est_cost_usd` back.
141	
142	**Quality.** A `QualityJudge` turns the final assistant message into a `[0,1]` score;
143	`grade_outcome` maps it to `success ≥ 0.8 / partial ≥ 0.4 / failure`. Defaults: `LLMJudge`
144	(claude-haiku, independent provider) when an Anthropic key is present, else a neutral
145	`ConstJudge(0.5)`. Pass `DeterministicJudge(your_fn)` for offline/cheap grading, or set
146	`HarnessConfig(judge_every=0)` to skip judging entirely (neutral `success` outcome, cost
147	still learned).
148	
149	**Candidate set + namespace.** `HarnessConfig.candidates` becomes Minima's
150	`Constraints.candidate_models`; `namespace` isolates memory (lane `minima:<namespace>`).
151	A fresh namespace = cold memory; use it to keep experiments clean.
152	
153	**Cost observability (CostMeter).** Hand a `CostMeter` to `MinimaAgent(..., meter=...)`
154	and it records one row per prompt — model picked, decision basis, est $ / actual $ /
155	savings-vs-baseline $, turns, quality, outcome — then `meter.report()` renders the table
156	plus totals (actual vs baseline $, **savings %**, success rate). The data already flowed
157	to Minima; the meter surfaces it to the human. `baseline_model_id` in `HarnessConfig`
158	populates each row's "save$" (resolved from the ranked set, no extra round-trip).
159	
160	**Override / veto (before_route).** Pass an `async (routing, task) -> RoutingResult | None`
161	hook to inspect or change Minima's pick *before* the model runs: return `None` to accept,
162	a modified `RoutingResult` to **override** the model/tier, or a result with
163	`recommendation_id=None` to **veto** (run a different model with no feedback attribution).
164	This is the explicit, auditable mitigation for routing's "silent quality regression" risk
165	and the seam for an interactive "Minima picked X for $Y because Z — accept?".
166	
167	**Code-quality-aware routing (the wedge).** Without signals, `recommend()` sees only the
168	prompt text, so Minima's recall is text-similarity-based. Pass `files=` to `prompt()` with
169	a `ContextExtractor` (default: `CodeHealthExtractor`) and the harness computes lightweight
170	code-health signals — proxy McCabe (decision-keyword count), non-blank LOC, sibling-test
171	detection, language-agnostic and dependency-free — and feeds them as `tags` /
172	`difficulty` / `expected_input_tokens` into the recommendation. Routing becomes
173	*code-aware*: a hard, untested refactor is steered toward a stronger tier than a trivial
174	extraction. A broken/missing extractor degrades gracefully to text-only routing. The
175	signal's discrimination (does it actually separate trivial/medium/complex tasks?) is the
176	falsifiable gate — see `tests/harness/test_signals.py`.
177	
178	**Token yield (iterations).** The agent loop records `state.turns_taken` per prompt; the
179	meter shows it and feedback sends it as `iterations` (a new, backward-compatible field on
180	`FeedbackRequest` / `OutcomeRecord`). This counters the "almost right" retry trap: a cheap
181	model that takes many turns to resolve can cost more than one frontier turn, and Minima can
182	now learn *tokens-to-resolution*, not just $/call.
183	
184	## Extending
185	
186	- **Add a provider** — implement the `Provider` protocol (`api_id` + async `stream()`
187	  yielding `Event`s) and `register_provider(api_id, instance())`. Reuse minima's optional
188	  extras (`anthropic`, `google-genai`); the OpenAI-compatible path uses raw `httpx` (no
189	  `openai` SDK).
190	- **Add a tool** — subclass the `AgentTool` shape: a pydantic `parameters` model + an
191	  async `execute(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult`. The loop
192	  validates args (errors become tool results the model can retry), runs tools in parallel
193	  (unless a tool opts into `execution_mode="sequential"`), and honours a `terminate=True`
194	  hint to skip the follow-up call.
195	- **Add a judge** — implement `QualityJudge.grade(task, output, *, rubric, expected) ->
196	  float` and pass it to `MinimaAgent(..., judge=...)`.
197	
198	## Testing
199	
200	```bash
201	uv run pytest tests/harness                 # hermetic (faux provider + FakeMemory + ASGI)
202	uv run pytest tests/harness -m live         # real providers (skip without keys)
203	uv run ruff check src/minima_harness && uv run mypy src/minima_harness
204	```
205	
206	The faux provider (`minima_harness.ai.providers.register_faux_provider`) gives hermetic
207	LLM calls; the full Minima round-trip is exercised in-process via `create_app` +
208	`FakeMemory` + an ASGI transport (see `tests/harness/test_minima_e2e.py`).
209	
210	## Build roadmap
211	
212	- **Phases 0–4** — scaffold → ported `pi-ai` (providers + compat) → ported
213	  `pi-agent-core` (agent loop + tools + hooks) → Minima integration (`MinimaAgent`) →
214	  example + these docs.
215	- **Phase A** — cost observability: `RoutingResult` carries Minima's full ranked/rationale/
216	  warnings payload + `baseline_cost_usd`; `CostMeter`; the `before_route` override/veto hook.
217	- **Phase B** — code-quality-aware routing: `CodeHealthExtractor` + `ContextExtractor`
218	  protocol; `tags`/`difficulty`/`expected_input_tokens` flow from touched files into
219	  `recommend()` via `MinimaAgent.prompt(files=...)`, with a discrimination gate.
220	- **Phase C** — token yield: `turns_taken` tracked per prompt → `feedback(iterations=)` →
221	  `FeedbackRequest`/`OutcomeRecord` gain a backward-compatible `iterations` field.
222	
223	See [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md) for the per-phase history.
224

file.content:
# Agent Harness (`minima_harness`)

A lean Python port of [`@earendil-works/pi`](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi)'s
agent toolkit (`pi-ai` + `pi-agent-core`), made **Minima-native**: every prompt is routed
through Minima, and every realized outcome (tokens / cost / latency / quality) is fed
back so Minima's memory sharpens. It lives alongside the Minima service in this repo at
[`src/minima_harness/`](../src/minima_harness/) and ships in the same wheel.

> The port is a from-scratch reimplementation of PI's design and APIs (no source copied);
> see `src/minima_harness/LICENSE_PI` for MIT attribution. The Minima integration layer is
> original work.

## The loop

```
MinimaAgent.prompt(task)
   │
   ├─ 1. ROUTE   ── MinimaRouter.recommend() ──▶ POST /v1/recommend
   │                sets agent.state.model (mapped from Minima's RankedModel)
   │
   ├─ 2. RUN     ── Agent loop (ported pi-agent-core)
   │                streams the model, executes tools, may run several turns
   │
   └─ 3. FEED BACK ─ MinimaRouter.feedback() ──▶ POST /v1/feedback
                     realized input_tokens / output_tokens / actual_cost_usd /
                     latency_ms + judged quality  ➜ outcome ∈ {success, partial, failure}
```

Minima never runs the model — the harness owns model dispatch (via the ported `ai`
provider layer) and quality judging. Routing is **bypassable**: if Minima is unreachable
and `allow_offline=True`, the run proceeds on the current model with no feedback.

## Architecture

```
src/minima_harness/
├── ai/                 ported pi-ai: unified multi-provider LLM API
│   ├── types.py          Model · Context · Message · ContentBlock · AssistantMessage · Usage
│   ├── registry.py       get_model/get_models/get_providers + seed catalog
│   ├── stream.py         stream() / complete()  (+ Stream wrapper with .result())
│   ├── tools.py          Tool + validate_tool_call  (pydantic params)
│   ├── usage.py          realized cost = tokens × model.price
│   ├── compat.py         cross-provider: foreign thinking → <thinking> text tags
│   └── providers/        base Protocol + faux (tests) · anthropic · google · openai_compat(httpx)
├── agent/              ported pi-agent-core: stateful agent runtime
│   ├── events.py         agent_start/end · turn_* · message_* · tool_execution_*
│   ├── tools.py          AgentTool · ToolResult · before/afterToolCall hooks
│   ├── state.py          AgentState + AgentLoopConfig
│   ├── loop.py           agent_loop async generator + tool execution (parallel via anyio)
│   └── agent.py          Agent: prompt/continue_/abort/wait_for_idle/subscribe/steer/follow_up
├── minima/             the Minima-native layer (original work)
│   ├── config.py         HarnessConfig (MINIMA_URL, candidates, namespace, judge policy)
│   ├── mapping.py        RankedModel → harness Model (tolerant: exact → id → provider/model)
│   ├── judge.py          QualityJudge Protocol · LLMJudge · DeterministicJudge · ConstJudge
│   ├── router.py         MinimaRouter: recommend → RoutingResult, feedback (realized cost)
│   └── runtime.py        MinimaAgent(Agent): route → run → judge → feedback per prompt
└── tasks/              task corpus + grade_outcome thresholds
    └── task_set.py        Task dataclass + sample tasks (absorbed from agent/task_set.py)
```

## Quickstart

### Install

```bash
pip install "minima-cli[harness,tui]"      # or: uv tool install "minima-cli[harness,tui]"
brew install mubit-ai/minima/minima         # Homebrew (see packaging/homebrew/README.md)
```

The command is **`minima`** (`minima-harness` stays as an alias). Then run `minima config`
to add your keys (see below).

### Demo mode (no keys, no services)

```bash
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py
```

Spins an in-process Minima (`FakeMemory`) + a fake Anthropic provider and runs the task
corpus through `MinimaAgent` end to end. Prints the per-task routing decision, quality,
outcome, realized tokens/cost, and how many outcomes landed in memory.

### Configure credentials (`minima config`)

For an installed CLI, store keys **per-user** instead of a per-directory `.env`:

```bash
minima config            # guided setup, two sections: LLM keys + Mubit/Minima
minima config list       # masked view of what's set + where (keyring | file)
minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY <key>
minima config doctor     # presence check + authenticated Minima probe (no secrets printed)
```

Secrets go to the **OS keyring** (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / libsecret)
when available, falling back to `~/.minima-harness/config.env` at mode `0600`. Non-secret
config (`MINIMA_URL`) always lives in the file. At launch the store is loaded into the
environment with **lowest precedence** — a shell `export`, a project `./.env.harness`, or
`./.env` still override it. The same surface is available in-TUI via `/config`.

Sections: **LLM provider keys** (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) ·
**Mubit / Minima routing** (`MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MINIMA_URL`, optional `MINIMA_API_KEY` →
falls back to `MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MUBIT_ENDPOINT`).

### Live mode (real Minima + real providers)

```bash
make run                       # Minima on :8080 in another terminal
export MUBIT_API_KEY=...       # passthrough auth on Minima
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...   # for the candidate models you allow
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live --rounds 3
```

### As a library

```python
import asyncio
from minima_harness.minima import HarnessConfig, MinimaAgent
from minima_harness.minima.judge import DeterministicJudge

async def main():
    agent = MinimaAgent(HarnessConfig.from_env())   # MINIMA_URL, MINIMA_API_KEY, candidates…
    # Optional: judge each output with your own scorer instead of the default LLM grader.
    agent.judge = DeterministicJudge(lambda output: 0.9 if "done" in output else 0.2)
    routing = await agent.prompt("Refactor foo() to be async.", task_type="code", slider=7)
    print(routing.chosen_model_id, routing.decision_basis)

asyncio.run(main())
```

## The integration points

**Routing granularity.** One recommend + one feedback per `prompt()`. A multi-turn
tool run uses the routed model throughout; each top-level prompt is one Minima outcome.
Call `prompt()` repeatedly in a conversation and every turn is routed and fed back
independently.

**Realized cost, not Minima's prior.** Feedback sends `actual_cost_usd = usage.cost.total`
computed from the provider's *actual* token counts × the harness registry prices. This is
what lets Minima climb `estimate → observed → rescaled` — its single biggest accuracy
lever. Do not echo Minima's `est_cost_usd` back.

**Quality.** A `QualityJudge` turns the final assistant message into a `[0,1]` score;
`grade_outcome` maps it to `success ≥ 0.8 / partial ≥ 0.4 / failure`. Defaults: `LLMJudge`
(claude-haiku, independent provider) when an Anthropic key is present, else a neutral
`ConstJudge(0.5)`. Pass `DeterministicJudge(your_fn)` for offline/cheap grading, or set
`HarnessConfig(judge_every=0)` to skip judging entirely (neutral `success` outcome, cost
still learned).

**Candidate set + namespace.** `HarnessConfig.candidates` becomes Minima's
`Constraints.candidate_models`; `namespace` isolates memory (lane `minima:<namespace>`).
A fresh namespace = cold memory; use it to keep experiments clean.

**Cost observability (CostMeter).** Hand a `CostMeter` to `MinimaAgent(..., meter=...)`
and it records one row per prompt — model picked, decision basis, est $ / actual $ /
savings-vs-baseline $, turns, quality, outcome — then `meter.report()` renders the table
plus totals (actual vs baseline $, **savings %**, success rate). The data already flowed
to Minima; the meter surfaces it to the human. `baseline_model_id` in `HarnessConfig`
populates each row's "save$" (resolved from the ranked set, no extra round-trip).

**Override / veto (before_route).** Pass an `async (routing, task) -> RoutingResult | None`
hook to inspect or change Minima's pick *before* the model runs: return `None` to accept,
a modified `RoutingResult` to **override** the model/tier, or a result with
`recommendation_id=None` to **veto** (run a different model with no feedback attribution).
This is the explicit, auditable mitigation for routing's "silent quality regression" risk
and the seam for an interactive "Minima picked X for $Y because Z — accept?".

**Code-quality-aware routing (the wedge).** Without signals, `recommend()` sees only the
prompt text, so Minima's recall is text-similarity-based. Pass `files=` to `prompt()` with
a `ContextExtractor` (default: `CodeHealthExtractor`) and the harness computes lightweight
code-health signals — proxy McCabe (decision-keyword count), non-blank LOC, sibling-test
detection, language-agnostic and dependency-free — and feeds them as `tags` /
`difficulty` / `expected_input_tokens` into the recommendation. Routing becomes
*code-aware*: a hard, untested refactor is steered toward a stronger tier than a trivial
extraction. A broken/missing extractor degrades gracefully to text-only routing. The
signal's discrimination (does it actually separate trivial/medium/complex tasks?) is the
falsifiable gate — see `tests/harness/test_signals.py`.

**Token yield (iterations).** The agent loop records `state.turns_taken` per prompt; the
meter shows it and feedback sends it as `iterations` (a new, backward-compatible field on
`FeedbackRequest` / `OutcomeRecord`). This counters the "almost right" retry trap: a cheap
model that takes many turns to resolve can cost more than one frontier turn, and Minima can
now learn *tokens-to-resolution*, not just $/call.

## Extending

- **Add a provider** — implement the `Provider` protocol (`api_id` + async `stream()`
  yielding `Event`s) and `register_provider(api_id, instance())`. Reuse minima's optional
  extras (`anthropic`, `google-genai`); the OpenAI-compatible path uses raw `httpx` (no
  `openai` SDK).
- **Add a tool** — subclass the `AgentTool` shape: a pydantic `parameters` model + an
  async `execute(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult`. The loop
  validates args (errors become tool results the model can retry), runs tools in parallel
  (unless a tool opts into `execution_mode="sequential"`), and honours a `terminate=True`
  hint to skip the follow-up call.
- **Add a judge** — implement `QualityJudge.grade(task, output, *, rubric, expected) ->
  float` and pass it to `MinimaAgent(..., judge=...)`.

## Testing

```bash
uv run pytest tests/harness                 # hermetic (faux provider + FakeMemory + ASGI)
uv run pytest tests/harness -m live         # real providers (skip without keys)
uv run ruff check src/minima_harness && uv run mypy src/minima_harness
```

The faux provider (`minima_harness.ai.providers.register_faux_provider`) gives hermetic
LLM calls; the full Minima round-trip is exercised in-process via `create_app` +
`FakeMemory` + an ASGI transport (see `tests/harness/test_minima_e2e.py`).

## Build roadmap

- **Phases 0–4** — scaffold → ported `pi-ai` (providers + compat) → ported
  `pi-agent-core` (agent loop + tools + hooks) → Minima integration (`MinimaAgent`) →
  example + these docs.
- **Phase A** — cost observability: `RoutingResult` carries Minima's full ranked/rationale/
  warnings payload + `baseline_cost_usd`; `CostMeter`; the `before_route` override/veto hook.
- **Phase B** — code-quality-aware routing: `CodeHealthExtractor` + `ContextExtractor`
  protocol; `tags`/`difficulty`/`expected_input_tokens` flow from touched files into
  `recommend()` via `MinimaAgent.prompt(files=...)`, with a discrimination gate.
- **Phase C** — token yield: `turns_taken` tracked per prompt → `feedback(iterations=)` →
  `FeedbackRequest`/`OutcomeRecord` gain a backward-compatible `iterations` field.

See [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md) for the per-phase history.

---

## Architectural Map: Minima Repository

Based on my thorough exploration, here's the comprehensive architectural overview:

### **1. Top-Level Directory Structure & Modules**

```
/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/
├── src/minima/                  # The Minima recommender service (FastAPI server)
│   └── [12 subdirs - see below]
├── src/minima_harness/          # Agent runtime with Minima integration
│   ├── ai/                      # Multi-provider LLM interface
│   ├── agent/                   # Stateful agent loop + tool execution
│   ├── minima/                  # Minima routing layer
│   ├── tools/                   # Built-in tools (read, write, bash, etc.)
│   ├── session/                 # Session management
│   ├── tasks/                   # Task corpus for evaluation
│   └── tui/                     # Terminal UI (Textual-based)
├── client_sdk/minima_client/    # Python HTTP client SDK
├── docs/                        # Full documentation
├── examples/                    # Runnable examples
├── tests/                       # Unit, integration, live, eval tests
└── packaging/                   # Brew/PyPI packaging
```

**Key dependencies**: `pydantic`, `httpx`, `anyio`, `anthropic`, `google-genai`, `mubit-sdk`, `fastapi` (server only)

---

### **2. The "Harness": What & Where**

**The harness** (`src/minima_harness/`) is a **stateful LLM agent runtime with built-in Minima routing**. It does **not** run Minima itself—it calls a running Minima service and feeds outcomes back.

#### Core Harness Loop:
```
MinimaAgent.prompt(task)
    ├─ 1. ROUTE via MinimaRouter.recommend()  → POST /v1/recommend to Minima
    ├─ 2. RUN   via agent_loop()               → stream model, execute tools, loop
    └─ 3. FEEDBACK via MinimaRouter.feedback() → POST /v1/feedback with realized cost/quality
```

**Key files:**
- **`runtime.py` (lines 1-150+)** — `MinimaAgent` class. Wraps the base `Agent`, adds routing/judging/cost-metering. Orchestrates the 3-phase loop.
- **`router.py` (lines 1-100+)** — `MinimaRouter` class. HTTP client to Minima. Maps Minima's `RankedModel` → harness `Model`. Handles offline fallback.
- **`config.py`** — `HarnessConfig`. Endpoint, candidates, judge model, namespace, slider.
- **`judge.py`** — `QualityJudge` protocol + implementations (`LLMJudge`, `DeterministicJudge`).
- **`meter.py`** — `CostMeter`. Tracks routing decisions, computes savings vs baseline.

**Base Agent** (`agent/agent.py` + `agent/agent_loop.py`):
- Async state machine: manages messages, tools, turns.
- Emits a rich event taxonomy for progress tracking.
- Executes tool calls in parallel (via `anyio`), handles tool hooks (before/after).
- Supports steering, follow-up injection, and early termination.

---

### **3. Routing & Escalation**

**Routing happens in `src/minima/recommender/`:**

**`engine.py` (lines 85-370)** — The main orchestrator:
1. **Classify** task (lines 90-131): heuristic + optional LLM refinement
2. **Select candidates** (line 102): apply constraints, pre-rank by capability
3. **Recall** (line 107-442): fetch similar past outcomes from Mubit
4. **Aggregate** (line 153): Beta-smoothed per-model success + optional IPW
5. **Score** (line 174-836): combine success + cost (estimate/observed/rescaled tiers)
6. **Optimize** (line 679-960): pick cheapest model clearing threshold τ
7. **Escalate** (line 214-246): consult cheap-LLM reasoner if evidence is thin/conflicting

**Escalation logic** (`escalation.py`):
- Triggers: thin evidence, low confidence, near threshold, tie, conflict
- Two modes: "legacy" (4 heuristics) vs "uncertainty" (interval-width gate)
- Consulted reasoner is a cheap LLM (Haiku/Flash) that ranks candidates with memory context
- Blend reasoner's estimate with deterministic (default 50/50)
- Decision basis logged: `memory`, `prior`, or `llm`

**Cost-basis tiers** (concepts in `score.py` + explained in `docs/concepts.md`):
- **Rescaled** (best): this request's input tokens + observed median output tokens
- **Observed**: median realized $/call from similar outcomes
- **Estimate** (cold): token estimate from catalog prices
- Single basis chosen for whole candidate set (all compared like-for-like)

---

### **4. LLM Provider Calls**

**Provider abstraction** (`src/minima_harness/ai/providers/`):
- **Protocol-based**: `Provider` base class defines `async stream(model, context, **opts) -> AsyncIterator[Event]`
- Implemented providers:
  - `anthropic.py` — Wraps `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic`, streams Messages API
  - `google.py` — Wraps `google.generativeai` async client
  - `openai_compat.py` — Raw `httpx.AsyncClient` for OpenAI-compatible endpoints
  - `faux.py` — Fake provider for testing (no API calls)
- **Registry** (`registry.py`): `get_model()`, `get_models()`, `get_providers()`

**For the Minima service itself:**
- **Reasoner** (`src/minima/llm/`): cheap-LLM esculator. Uses Anthropic/Gemini SDK (configured via `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER`).
- **LLM classifier**: optional LLM-based task classification when heuristic is uncertain.

**HTTP clients:**
- `httpx.AsyncClient` used for:
  - Catalog fetching (LiteLLM prices, OpenRouter models)
  - Mubit memory recall/lookup (via `minima/memory/adapter.py`)
  - Custom endpoints (no SDK available)

---

### **5. Tools & Tool-Use Execution**

**Tool definition** (`agent/tools.py`):
```python
@dataclass
class AgentTool:
    name: str
    description: str
    parameters: type[BaseModel]  # Pydantic model for validation
    execute: ToolExecute  # async (tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult
    execution_mode: "parallel" | "sequential" | None
```

**Built-in tools** (`tools/` directory):
- `read_tool()` — read files with line-based offset/limit
- `write_tool()` — create files
- `edit_tool()` — in-place text edits
- `bash_tool()` — run shell commands with timeout
- `grep_tool()` — regex search with file paths
- `find_tool()` — file search
- `ls_tool()` — directory listing
- `tasks_tool()` — fetch/update goals from a store

**Tool execution loop** (`agent/loop.py` lines 96-111):
1. Model streams; if `stop_reason == "toolUse"`, extract tool calls
2. Validate params against pydantic model (errors → tool error results)
3. Execute tools in parallel (via `anyio.create_task_group()`) unless `execution_mode="sequential"`
4. Run **before/afterToolCall** hooks (can block, modify, or transform results)
5. Append `ToolResult` messages
6. Loop continues unless all results have `terminate=True`

**No native "websearch" tool yet** — this is where you'd add the capability.

---

### **6. Languages, Dependencies, Entry Points**

**Language**: Python 3.11+ (tested on 3.11, 3.12, 3.13)

**Key runtime dependencies**:
- `pydantic>=2.7` — validation, serialization
- `httpx>=0.27` — async HTTP
- `anyio>=4.4` — async task groups, cancellation
- `structlog>=24.1` — structured logging
- `tenacity>=8.4` — retry logic
- `anthropic>=0.40` — Anthropic SDK (optional: `[harness]`, `[reasoner-anthropic]`)
- `google-genai>=0.3` — Google Gemini (optional: `[harness]`, `[reasoner-gemini]`)
- `mubit-sdk>=0.10.0` — Mubit memory client

**Server extras** (for self-hosted Minima):
- `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `psycopg2-binary`, `redis`

**Entry points**:
```
minima / minima-harness       → src/minima_harness/tui/cli.py:main  (Terminal UI)
minima-seed                   → src/minima/seeding/run_seed.py:main
minima-calibration-report     → src/minima/metrics/report.py:main
```

**Run the service:**
```bash
make run                      # uvicorn on :8080 (minima.main:app)
make run-mubit                # Mubit runtime (separate repo) on :3000
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py [--live]
```

---

### **7. Existing External API Integration**

**HTTP/API patterns already in codebase:**

1. **Catalog refreshing** (`src/minima/catalog/sources/`):
   - `litellm.py` — `async def fetch_litellm_prices(url)` using `httpx.AsyncClient`
   - `openrouter.py` — `async def fetch_openrouter_models()` via httpx
   - Both use timeout + error handling with fallback

2. **Mubit memory adapter** (`src/minima/memory/adapter.py`):
   - Wraps `mubit_sdk` (blocking SDK) with async thread pool
   - Calls: `recall()`, `lookup()`, `dereference()`, `get_context()` → all are Mubit RPCs

3. **Tool-execution streaming** (`tools/bash.py` example):
   - Subprocess stdout streamed via `on_update()` callback
   - Reusable pattern for any streaming async operation

4. **Reasoner HTTP calls**: Implicit via SDK (Anthropic/Google client libraries handle the wire)

**No existing web-search integration** — the pattern to follow:
- Define a `WebSearchParams` pydantic model (URL, query, max results, timeout)
- Implement `async def _execute(...)` using `httpx.AsyncClient`
- Wrap in `AgentTool(name="websearch", execute=_execute, ...)`
- Register in `default_toolset()` or pass to `MinimaAgent(..., tools=[websearch_tool(), ...])`

---

### **8. Architecture Summary Diagram**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      Your LLM Workflow                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  MinimaAgent (minima_harness/minima/runtime.py)                │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ prompt(task, task_type, slider, files, tags)           │   │
│  │  ├─ 1. route: POST /v1/recommend → MinimaRouter        │   │
│  │  ├─ 2. run: agent_loop(prompts) → provider stream()    │   │

---

### **Key Insights for Designing WebSearch Integration**

1. **Where to add it**: New file `src/minima_harness/tools/websearch.py` (parallel to `bash.py`, `read.py`)

2. **Tool structure** (follow `bash.py` pattern):
   ```python
   class WebSearchParams(BaseModel):
       query: str
       max_results: int = 5
       timeout_ms: int = 10000
   
   async def _execute(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update):
       # Use httpx.AsyncClient for HTTP requests
       # Stream progress via on_update() if applicable
       # Return ToolResult with [TextContent(...)]
   
   def websearch_tool() -> AgentTool:
       return AgentTool(
           name="websearch",
           description="Search the web for recent information",
           parameters=WebSearchParams,
           execute=_execute,
       )
   ```

3. **Integration point**: Add to `default_toolset()` in `tools/builtin.py` or pass to `MinimaAgent(..., tools=[websearch_tool()])`

4. **Routing considerations**:
   - Tasks with `task_type="tool_use"` or tags like `"requires:websearch"` can be routed with higher quality threshold
   - Routing is **per prompt**, so each agent turn gets independently routed and fed back
   - Minima learns which models handle tool-use tasks well over time

5. **No hooks needed**: Tools don't need Minima integration—they're purely agent-side. The agent loop + feedback naturally learn which models + tool combinations work best.

---

This should give you a complete picture of the harness architecture and where websearch fits into the tool ecosystem.</result>

### **4. LLM Provider Calls**

**Provider abstraction** (`src/minima_harness/ai/providers/`):
- **Protocol-based**: `Provider` base class defines `async stream(model, context, **opts) -> AsyncIterator[Event]`
- Implemented providers:
  - `anthropic.py` — Wraps `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic`, streams Messages API
  - `google.py` — Wraps `google.generativeai` async client
  - `openai_compat.py` — Raw `httpx.AsyncClient` for OpenAI-compatible endpoints
  - `faux.py` — Fake provider for testing (no API calls)
- **Registry** (`registry.py`): `get_model()`, `get_models()`, `get_providers()`

**For the Minima service itself:**
- **Reasoner** (`src/minima/llm/`): cheap-LLM esculator. Uses Anthropic/Gemini SDK (configured via `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER`).
- **LLM classifier**: optional LLM-based task classification when heuristic is uncertain.

**HTTP clients:**
- `httpx.AsyncClient` used for:
  - Catalog fetching (LiteLLM prices, OpenRouter models)
  - Mubit memory recall/lookup (via `minima/memory/adapter.py`)
  - Custom endpoints (no SDK available)

---

### **5. Tools & Tool-Use Execution**

**Tool definition** (`agent/tools.py`):
```python
@dataclass
class AgentTool:
    name: str
    description: str
    parameters: type[BaseModel]  # Pydantic model for validation
    execute: ToolExecute  # async (tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult
    execution_mode: "parallel" | "sequential" | None
```

**Built-in tools** (`tools/` directory):
- `read_tool()` — read files with line-based offset/limit
- `write_tool()` — create files
- `edit_tool()` — in-place text edits
- `bash_tool()` — run shell commands with timeout
- `grep_tool()` — regex search with file paths
- `find_tool()` — file search
- `ls_tool()` — directory listing
- `tasks_tool()` — fetch/update goals from a store

**Tool execution loop** (`agent/loop.py` lines 96-111):
1. Model streams; if `stop_reason == "toolUse"`, extract tool calls
2. Validate params against pydantic model (errors → tool error results)
3. Execute tools in parallel (via `anyio.create_task_group()`) unless `execution_mode="sequential"`
4. Run **before/afterToolCall** hooks (can block, modify, or transform results)
5. Append `ToolResult` messages
6. Loop continues unless all results have `terminate=True`

**No native "websearch" tool yet** — this is where you'd add the capability.

---

### **6. Languages, Dependencies, Entry Points**

**Language**: Python 3.11+ (tested on 3.11, 3.12, 3.13)

**Key runtime dependencies**:
- `pydantic>=2.7` — validation, serialization
- `httpx>=0.27` — async HTTP
- `anyio>=4.4` — async task groups, cancellation
- `structlog>=24.1` — structured logging
- `tenacity>=8.4` — retry logic
- `anthropic>=0.40` — Anthropic SDK (optional: `[harness]`, `[reasoner-anthropic]`)
- `google-genai>=0.3` — Google Gemini (optional: `[harness]`, `[reasoner-gemini]`)
- `mubit-sdk>=0.10.0` — Mubit memory client

**Server extras** (for self-hosted Minima):
- `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `psycopg2-binary`, `redis`

**Entry points**:
```
minima / minima-harness       → src/minima_harness/tui/cli.py:main  (Terminal UI)
minima-seed                   → src/minima/seeding/run_seed.py:main
minima-calibration-report     → src/minima/metrics/report.py:main
```

**Run the service:**
```bash
make run                      # uvicorn on :8080 (minima.main:app)
make run-mubit                # Mubit runtime (separate repo) on :3000
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py [--live]
```

---

### **7. Existing External API Integration**

**HTTP/API patterns already in codebase:**

1. **Catalog refreshing** (`src/minima/catalog/sources/`):
   - `litellm.py` — `async def fetch_litellm_prices(url)` using `httpx.AsyncClient`
   - `openrouter.py` — `async def fetch_openrouter_models()` via httpx
   - Both use timeout + error handling with fallback

2. **Mubit memory adapter** (`src/minima/memory/adapter.py`):
   - Wraps `mubit_sdk` (blocking SDK) with async thread pool
   - Calls: `recall()`, `lookup()`, `dereference()`, `get_context()` → all are Mubit RPCs

3. **Tool-execution streaming** (`tools/bash.py` example):
   - Subprocess stdout streamed via `on_update()` callback
   - Reusable pattern for any streaming async operation

4. **Reasoner HTTP calls**: Implicit via SDK (Anthropic/Google client libraries handle the wire)

**No existing web-search integration** — the pattern to follow:
- Define a `WebSearchParams` pydantic model (URL, query, max results, timeout)
- Implement `async def _execute(...)` using `httpx.AsyncClient`
- Wrap in `AgentTool(name="websearch", execute=_execute, ...)`
- Register in `default_toolset()` or pass to `MinimaAgent(..., tools=[websearch_tool(), ...])`

---

### **8. Architecture Summary Diagram**

```

---

### **Key Insights for Designing WebSearch Integration**

1. **Where to add it**: New file `src/minima_harness/tools/websearch.py` (parallel to `bash.py`, `read.py`)

2. **Tool structure** (follow `bash.py` pattern):
   ```python
   class WebSearchParams(BaseModel):
       query: str
       max_results: int = 5
       timeout_ms: int = 10000
   
   async def _execute(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update):
       # Use httpx.AsyncClient for HTTP requests
       # Stream progress via on_update() if applicable
       # Return ToolResult with [TextContent(...)]
   
   def websearch_tool() -> AgentTool:
       return AgentTool(
           name="websearch",
           description="Search the web for recent information",
           parameters=WebSearchParams,
           execute=_execute,
       )
   ```

3. **Integration point**: Add to `default_toolset()` in `tools/builtin.py` or pass to `MinimaAgent(..., tools=[websearch_tool()])`

4. **Routing considerations**:
   - Tasks with `task_type="tool_use"` or tags like `"requires:websearch"` can be routed with higher quality threshold
   - Routing is **per prompt**, so each agent turn gets independently routed and fed back
   - Minima learns which models handle tool-use tasks well over time

5. **No hooks needed**: Tools don't need Minima integration—they're purely agent-side. The agent loop + feedback naturally learn which models + tool combinations work best.

---

This should give you a complete picture of the harness architecture and where websearch fits into the tool ecosystem.</result>

file.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from pathlib import Path

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

from minima_harness.agent.tools import AgentTool, ToolResult, ToolUpdate, error_result
from minima_harness.ai.types import TextContent


class BashParams(BaseModel):
    command: str
    timeout: int = Field(default=120_000, ge=1)  # milliseconds
    workdir: str | None = None


async def _execute(
    tool_call_id: str,
    params,
    signal,
    on_update: ToolUpdate | None,  # noqa: ANN001
) -> ToolResult:
    assert isinstance(params, BashParams)
    wd = str(Path(params.workdir).expanduser()) if params.workdir else None
    try:
        proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(
            params.command,
            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.STDOUT,
            cwd=wd,
            start_new_session=True,
        )
    except OSError as exc:
        return error_result(f"bash: failed to start: {exc}")

    chunks: list[str] = []
    assert proc.stdout is not None
    try:
        async with asyncio.timeout(params.timeout / 1000.0):
            async for raw in proc.stdout:
                chunk = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
                chunks.append(chunk)
                if on_update is not None:
                    try:
                        on_update(chunk)
                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - progress must never break the run
                        pass
            await proc.wait()
    except TimeoutError:
        proc.kill()
        await proc.wait()
        return error_result(f"bash: timed out after {params.timeout} ms")

    output = "".join(chunks)
    code = proc.returncode if proc.returncode is not None else -1
    body = f"{output}\n[exit {code}]" if output else f"[exit {code}]"
    return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text=body)], details={"exit_code": code})


def bash_tool() -> AgentTool:
    return AgentTool(
        name="bash",
        description=(
            "Run a shell command and return its combined stdout/stderr and exit code. "
            "Output streams live. Runs with the user's full permissions — no confirmation."
        ),
        parameters=BashParams,
        execute=_execute,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from minima_harness.agent.tools import AgentTool
from minima_harness.tools.bash import bash_tool
from minima_harness.tools.edit import edit_tool
from minima_harness.tools.find import find_tool
from minima_harness.tools.grep import grep_tool
from minima_harness.tools.ls import ls_tool
from minima_harness.tools.read import read_tool
from minima_harness.tools.write import write_tool


def default_toolset() -> list[AgentTool]:
    """PI's seven default coding tools, in a stable order."""
    return [
        read_tool(),
        write_tool(),
        edit_tool(),
        bash_tool(),
        grep_tool(),
        find_tool(),
        ls_tool(),
    ]

file.content:
"""Agent tools and execution hooks — port of PI's ``AgentTool`` + before/afterToolCall.

Tools declare parameters as a pydantic model (the TypeBox analogue); ``execute`` is an
async callable ``(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult``. Validation
errors and thrown exceptions become tool-error results fed back to the model so it can
retry (matching PI).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel

from minima_harness.ai.types import ContentBlock, TextContent, ToolCall

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from minima_harness.agent.state import AgentState

ToolExecutionMode = Literal["parallel", "sequential"]
ThinkingLevel = Literal["off", "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]

# on_update(partial_result) -> None; called mid-execution for streaming progress.
ToolUpdate = Callable[[Any], None]
ToolExecute = Callable[[str, BaseModel, object | None, ToolUpdate | None], Awaitable["ToolResult"]]


@dataclass(slots=True)
class ToolResult:
    """What a tool returns. ``content`` goes to the model; ``details`` are app-facing."""

    content: list[ContentBlock]
    details: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    # Hint to skip the automatic follow-up LLM call. Only honoured when every finalized
    # tool result in the batch also sets terminate=True.
    terminate: bool = False


@dataclass(slots=True)
class AgentTool:
    name: str
    description: str
    parameters: type[BaseModel]
    execute: ToolExecute
    execution_mode: ToolExecutionMode | None = None
    label: str = ""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hook types
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@dataclass(slots=True)
class BeforeToolCallContext:
    tool_call: ToolCall
    args: BaseModel  # validated params
    context: AgentState


@dataclass(slots=True)
class BeforeToolCallResult:
    block: bool = False
    reason: str = ""


@dataclass(slots=True)
class AfterToolCallContext:
    tool_call: ToolCall
    result: ToolResult
    is_error: bool
    context: AgentState


@dataclass(slots=True)
class AfterToolCallResult:
    terminate: bool = False
    details: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    content: list[ContentBlock] | None = None


BeforeToolCall = Callable[[BeforeToolCallContext], Awaitable[BeforeToolCallResult | None]]
AfterToolCall = Callable[[AfterToolCallContext], Awaitable[AfterToolCallResult | None]]


def find_agent_tool(tools: list[AgentTool], name: str) -> AgentTool | None:
    for t in tools:
        if t.name == name:
            return t
    return None


def error_result(message: str) -> ToolResult:
    """A standard error tool result (single text block)."""
    return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text=message)])

content.text:
This is exactly the right way to approach it — build the feature as a sequence of small, self-contained projects. I've now mapped your harness so the guide will reference real files (`src/minima_harness/tools/*.py`, the `AgentTool` dataclass, `default_toolset()`, the `httpx`-based patterns already used in `catalog/sources/`).

Before I write the guide, a few decisions genuinely shape what you'll build and learn. Let me ask.

questions.question: Which web-search backend should the guide target? This is the biggest fork — it changes what fundamentals you learn.

options.label: Third-party search API (Recommended)
options.description: Use a search API like Tavily, Brave Search, Exa, or SerpAPI. You call an HTTP endpoint, get back ranked results. Teaches API integration, httpx, auth/keys, rate limits, result parsing — and matches your existing catalog/sources httpx pattern.

options.description: Use Anthropic's server-side web_search tool (the model searches during generation, no separate tool execution). Less to build, but provider-specific and skips a lot of the plumbing fundamentals.

options.label: Build it yourself (fetch + parse)
options.description: Hit a search engine / fetch raw pages and parse HTML yourself (httpx + selectolax/BeautifulSoup). Most fundamentals, most fragile, most work. Good pure learning, less practical.

-> parse with selectolax
-> extract links/text

Tool 2: web_fetch(url) -> clean text

Model chains them in the loop

 -> [{title, url, snippet}]

Simplest viable feature

options.label: Pure tool first, then integrate (Recommended)
options.description: Build & ship the web-search tool as a normal AgentTool first (gets you a working feature fast). Add a later project on routing implications (task_type tagging, feedback signals for search-heavy tasks).
options.preview:
Phase 1: working tool

Incremental, lower risk

options.preview:
Just the tool, done well.
No routing changes.

options.label: Integrate from the start
options.description: Design the tool with Minima routing/feedback in mind from project one. More conceptual load up front for a junior dev.

Tool + routing designed
together from day one

"""Fetch the LiteLLM community price map (per-token costs for thousands of models)."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

import httpx


async def fetch_litellm_prices(url: str, timeout: float = 20.0) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Return the raw ``{model_key: {...cost fields...}}`` map.

    Raises on network/HTTP error; callers treat failure as "keep last-good catalog".
    """
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=timeout) as client:
        resp = await client.get(url)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
    return data if isinstance(data, dict) else {}
