from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import asyncio
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from minima_harness.minima.config import HarnessConfig
from minima_harness.minima.meter import CostMeter
from minima_harness.minima.runtime import MinimaAgent
from minima_harness.session import SessionManager, SessionStore
from minima_harness.tui.app import HarnessApp
from minima_harness.tui.context import build_system_prompt
from minima_harness.tui.packages import packages_cli

# .env files (in cwd) auto-loaded so `minima-harness` works without `make`/`--env-file`.
_ENV_FILES = (".env.harness", ".env")
_PKG_COMMANDS = ("install", "list", "remove")


def _load_env_files() -> None:
    for name in _ENV_FILES:
        path = Path(name)
        if not path.is_file():
            continue
        for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
                continue
            key, _, val = line.partition("=")
            val = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
            os.environ.setdefault(key.strip(), val)  # real env / --env-file wins
    # Per-user store (OS keyring + ~/.minima-harness/config.env) — lowest precedence, so the
    # CLI works from any directory while shell env and project .env files still override it.
    try:
        from minima_harness.tui.config_store import hydrate_env

        hydrate_env()
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - config must never block startup
        pass


def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="minima", description="Minima CLI — cost-aware model-routing coding agent."
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "prompt", nargs="*", help="optional initial prompt (used by --print/--mode json)"
    )
    p.add_argument("--provider")
    p.add_argument("--model")
    p.add_argument("--thinking", default="off")
    p.add_argument("-c", "--continue", dest="continue_last", action="store_true")
    p.add_argument("-r", "--resume", action="store_true")
    p.add_argument("--session")
    p.add_argument("--fork")
    p.add_argument("--no-session", action="store_true")
    p.add_argument("-n", "--name")
    p.add_argument("-t", "--tools", help="comma-separated allowlist")
    p.add_argument("-xt", "--exclude-tools", help="comma-separated denylist")
    p.add_argument("-nt", "--no-tools", action="store_true")
    p.add_argument("--offline", action="store_true")
    p.add_argument("-p", "--print", action="store_true", help="one-shot: print the reply and exit")
    p.add_argument(
        "--mode",
        choices=("interactive", "print", "json"),
        default="interactive",
        help="run mode (interactive TUI, one-shot print, or JSON event stream)",
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "--mouse",
        action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction,
        default=None,  # resolved per-terminal below (see _resolve_mouse)
        help="capture the mouse: scroll-wheel + in-app drag-select & copy. Default ON, except "
        "macOS Terminal.app — it doesn't report mouse motion (xterm 1003), so in-app drag-select "
        "can't work and capture would only block its native selection; defaults OFF there (select "
        "natively, scroll with PageUp/PageDown). Override with --mouse/--no-mouse; /mouse toggles.",
    )
    p.add_argument(

content.content:
=== config_cli required keys ===
def _interactive() -> int:
    print("minima config — press Enter to keep the current value.\n")
    for section in store.SECTIONS:
        print(f"# {section.title} — {section.note}")
        for f in section.fields:
            cur = store.get(f.key)
            if f.secret:
                shown = store.mask(cur) if cur else "unset"
                entered = getpass.getpass(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            else:
                shown = cur or f.default or "unset"
                entered = input(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            if entered:
                print(f"    saved → {store.set_value(f.key, entered)}")
            elif not cur and f.default and not f.secret:
                store.set_value(f.key, f.default)
                print(f"    saved default → {f.default}")
        print()
    print("done. Run `minima config doctor` to verify.")
    return 0


def _doctor() -> int:
    store.hydrate_env()
    print("config doctor\n")
    providers = [
        ("Anthropic", "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"),
        ("Gemini", "GEMINI_API_KEY"),
        ("OpenAI", "OPENAI_API_KEY"),
        ("Mubit", "MUBIT_API_KEY"),
    ]
    for label, key in providers:
        ok = bool(os.environ.get(key))
        print(f"  [{'ok' if ok else '  '}] {label:<10} {key:<18} {'present' if ok else 'missing'}")

    url = os.environ.get("MINIMA_URL", DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL)
    print(f"\n  Minima endpoint: {url}")
    import httpx

    reachable = False
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/health", timeout=5.0)
        print(f"  health: HTTP {resp.status_code}")
        reachable = True
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any failure is just 'unreachable'
        print(f"  health: unreachable ({type(exc).__name__})")

    # Authenticated probe: /v1/health is unauthenticated, so it can't tell a valid key from a
    # bad one. A minimal recommend with the key actually exercises the routing auth path.
    key = os.environ.get("MINIMA_API_KEY") or os.environ.get("MUBIT_API_KEY")
    if not reachable:
        return 0
    if not key:
        print("  auth: no MUBIT_API_KEY/MINIMA_API_KEY set — routing falls back to offline")
        return 0
    try:
        r = httpx.post(
            url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/recommend",
            headers={"authorization": f"Bearer {key}"},
            json={"task": {"task": "minima config doctor probe"}, "cost_quality_tradeoff": 5},
            timeout=15.0,
        )
        if r.status_code == 200:
            print("  auth: OK — key accepted")
        elif r.status_code in (401, 403):
            print(f"  auth: FAILED — key rejected (HTTP {r.status_code})")
        else:
            print(f"  auth: HTTP {r.status_code}")
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        print(f"  auth: probe failed ({type(exc).__name__})")
    return 0

=== config_cli required keys ===
def _interactive() -> int:
    print("minima config — press Enter to keep the current value.\n")
    for section in store.SECTIONS:
        print(f"# {section.title} — {section.note}")
        for f in section.fields:
            cur = store.get(f.key)
            if f.secret:
                shown = store.mask(cur) if cur else "unset"
                entered = getpass.getpass(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            else:
                shown = cur or f.default or "unset"
                entered = input(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            if entered:
                print(f"    saved → {store.set_value(f.key, entered)}")
            elif not cur and f.default and not f.secret:
                store.set_value(f.key, f.default)
                print(f"    saved default → {f.default}")
        print()
    print("done. Run `minima config doctor` to verify.")
    return 0


def _doctor() -> int:
    store.hydrate_env()
    print("config doctor\n")
    providers = [
        ("Anthropic", "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"),
        ("Gemini", "GEMINI_API_KEY"),
        ("OpenAI", "OPENAI_API_KEY"),
        ("Mubit", "MUBIT_API_KEY"),
    ]
    for label, key in providers:
        ok = bool(os.environ.get(key))
        print(f"  [{'ok' if ok else '  '}] {label:<10} {key:<18} {'present' if ok else 'missing'}")

    url = os.environ.get("MINIMA_URL", DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL)
    print(f"\n  Minima endpoint: {url}")
    import httpx

    reachable = False
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/health", timeout=5.0)
        print(f"  health: HTTP {resp.status_code}")
        reachable = True
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any failure is just 'unreachable'
        print(f"  health: unreachable ({type(exc).__name__})")

    # Authenticated probe: /v1/health is unauthenticated, so it can't tell a valid key from a
    # bad one. A minimal recommend with the key actually exercises the routing auth path.
    key = os.environ.get("MINIMA_API_KEY") or os.environ.get("MUBIT_API_KEY")
    if not reachable:
        return 0
    if not key:
        print("  auth: no MUBIT_API_KEY/MINIMA_API_KEY set — routing falls back to offline")
        return 0
    try:
        r = httpx.post(
            url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/recommend",
            headers={"authorization": f"Bearer {key}"},
            json={"task": {"task": "minima config doctor probe"}, "cost_quality_tradeoff": 5},
            timeout=15.0,
        )
        if r.status_code == 200:
            print("  auth: OK — key accepted")
        elif r.status_code in (401, 403):
            print(f"  auth: FAILED — key rejected (HTTP {r.status_code})")
        else:
            print(f"  auth: HTTP {r.status_code}")
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        print(f"  auth: probe failed ({type(exc).__name__})")
    return 0

aiTitle: Build websearch capabilities for Minima harness

content.text:
You're almost fully set up — `.env.harness` already has your `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MUBIT_API_KEY`, and `MINIMA_URL`. The only thing missing is `EXA_API_KEY`. Here's the complete runbook.

## 1. Insert your Exa API key

The harness CLI auto-loads `.env.harness` (then `.env`) from the repo root at startup — and both are already gitignored (`.env.*` in `.gitignore`), so your key won't be committed. `.env.harness` is loaded **first**, so that's the right place.

Add one line to `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/.env.harness`:

```
EXA_API_KEY=[REDACTED]
```

Quick way (replace with your real key):
```bash
echo 'EXA_API_KEY=[REDACTED]' >> .env.harness
```

**Precedence** (highest wins): shell `export` → `--env-file` → `.env.harness` → `.env` → per-user config store. So a `export EXA_API_KEY=[REDACTED] in your shell always overrides the file if you need to.

## 2. Start the harness to see your changes

Your changes live in the **harness agent + tools** (`src/minima_harness/`). The agent normally routes through a running Minima *service*, but you don't need to stand that up just to exercise the tools — use `--offline`, which makes the router fall back to local model selection while the agent (and your `web_search`/`web_fetch` tools) run normally.

**Simplest — no service needed:**
```bash
uv run minima --offline
```
This opens the interactive TUI. Since I added the web tools to `default_toolset()`, they're available automatically.

**Full routing (optional)** — if you also want the real route→run→feedback loop, start the service in a second terminal first:
```bash
make run            # terminal 1: Minima service on :8080
uv run minima       # terminal 2: harness routes to it
```

**Verify keys are seen** (Exa isn't in `config doctor`, but this confirms the provider/routing side):
```bash
uv run minima config doctor
```

## 3. Trigger the web tools

In the TUI, give it a task that needs fresh info — for example:

> Search the web for the latest stable Python release, then fetch the release page and cite the URL.

You should see it call `web_search` → then `web_fetch` on a result. To **force focus** on just your tools (good for a first test), use the allowlist flag:

```bash
uv run minima --offline -t web_search,web_fetch
```

Or run it one-shot without the interactive UI:
```bash
uv run minima --offline -p "search the web for the latest stable Python release and cite a URL"
```

If `web_search` returns `web_search failed: EXA_API_KEY is not set`, the key didn't load — confirm the line is in `.env.harness` and you launched `minima` **from the repo root** (the `.env` auto-load reads the current working directory).

Want me to append the `EXA_API_KEY=` line to `.env.harness` now (you'd paste in the value), or are you good to add it yourself?

content: You're building web search for the Minima harness; the Exa-backed web_search and web_fetch tools are built, registered, and passing all 20 tests. Next, add your EXA_API_KEY to .env.harness and run `uv run minima --offline` to try them. (disable recaps in /config)

aiTitle: Build websearch capabilities for Minima harness

content.text:
Those screenshots tell a clear story, and it's not what you'd hope: **the model never called `web_search` or `web_fetch` at all.** Every line is `bash` — it tried to scrape the web by hand with `curl`, then `lynx`, `elinks`, `w3m`, `sudo apt-get install lynx`, `pip install html2text`, `pip install pup`… got binary gibberish back from `curl` (compressed HTML), flailed for dozens of steps, and finally hallucinated a wrong answer (it said 3.13.1 / 3.12.3 — the real latest is **3.14.6**, which your tools returned correctly in our live test).

So those "downloads and updates" are the **model itself** running install/download commands inside `bash`, not the harness. Let me look at how tools and the system prompt are presented so I can fix the root cause: the model preferring `bash` over your web tools.

content.content:
1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from pathlib import Path
4	
5	from minima_harness.session.format import EntryType
6	
7	CONTEXT_FILES = ("AGENTS.md", "CLAUDE.md")
8	GLOBAL_DIR = Path.home() / ".minima-harness"
9	
10	BASE_SYSTEM = (
11	    "You are an interactive coding agent running in the user's terminal. Use the provided "
12	    "tools (read, write, edit, bash, grep, find, ls) to explore and modify the codebase. "
13	    "Be concise and direct; explain only when asked."
14	)
15	
16	SUMMARY_SYSTEM = (
17	    "You compact a coding-agent conversation. Summarize the work done so far: key decisions, "
18	    "file paths touched, current state, and open questions. Be concise. Output only the summary."
19	)
20	SUMMARY_USER = "Summarize the conversation above for context continuity."
21	
22	
23	def _read(path: Path) -> str | None:
24	    try:
25	        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
26	        return text.strip() or None
27	    except OSError:  # noqa: BLE001
28	        return None
29	
30	
31	def load_agents_md(cwd: Path) -> str:
32	    """Concatenate AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md from the global dir + cwd's parent chain (root → cwd)."""
33	    chunks: list[str] = []
34	    for name in CONTEXT_FILES:
35	        g = _read(GLOBAL_DIR / name)
36	        if g:
37	            chunks.append(f"# ({name}, global)\n{g}")
38	
39	    parts: list[Path] = []
40	    node = cwd.resolve()
41	    while True:
42	        parts.append(node)
43	        if node.parent == node:
44	            break
45	        node = node.parent
46	    for d in reversed(parts):  # rootward first, cwd last
47	        for name in CONTEXT_FILES:
48	            t = _read(d / name)
49	            if t:
50	                chunks.append(f"# ({name}, {d.name})\n{t}")
51	    return "\n\n".join(chunks)
52	
53	
54	def build_system_prompt_parts(cwd: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
55	    """The local system prompt as labeled parts: always ``("base", ...)``, plus
56	    ``("agents.md", ...)`` when project context exists.
57	
58	    ``build_system_prompt`` joins these into one string; splitting them lets the prompt
59	    inspector show the base prompt and project context (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) as distinct
60	    layers without duplicating the assembly logic.
61	    """
62	    replace = _read(cwd / "SYSTEM.md") or _read(GLOBAL_DIR / "SYSTEM.md")
63	    append = _read(cwd / "APPEND_SYSTEM.md") or _read(GLOBAL_DIR / "APPEND_SYSTEM.md")
64	    base = replace if replace else BASE_SYSTEM
65	    if append:
66	        base = f"{base}\n\n{append}"
67	    parts: list[tuple[str, str]] = [("base", base)]
68	    agents = load_agents_md(cwd)

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from minima_harness.session.format import EntryType

CONTEXT_FILES = ("AGENTS.md", "CLAUDE.md")
GLOBAL_DIR = Path.home() / ".minima-harness"

BASE_SYSTEM = (
    "You are an interactive coding agent running in the user's terminal. Use the provided "
    "tools (read, write, edit, bash, grep, find, ls) to explore and modify the codebase. "
    "Be concise and direct; explain only when asked."
)

SUMMARY_SYSTEM = (
    "You compact a coding-agent conversation. Summarize the work done so far: key decisions, "
    "file paths touched, current state, and open questions. Be concise. Output only the summary."
)
SUMMARY_USER = "Summarize the conversation above for context continuity."


def _read(path: Path) -> str | None:
    try:
        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        return text.strip() or None
    except OSError:  # noqa: BLE001
        return None


def load_agents_md(cwd: Path) -> str:
    """Concatenate AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md from the global dir + cwd's parent chain (root → cwd)."""
    chunks: list[str] = []
    for name in CONTEXT_FILES:
        g = _read(GLOBAL_DIR / name)
        if g:
            chunks.append(f"# ({name}, global)\n{g}")

    parts: list[Path] = []
    node = cwd.resolve()
    while True:
        parts.append(node)
        if node.parent == node:
            break
        node = node.parent
    for d in reversed(parts):  # rootward first, cwd last
        for name in CONTEXT_FILES:
            t = _read(d / name)
            if t:
                chunks.append(f"# ({name}, {d.name})\n{t}")
    return "\n\n".join(chunks)


def build_system_prompt_parts(cwd: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """The local system prompt as labeled parts: always ``("base", ...)``, plus
    ``("agents.md", ...)`` when project context exists.

    ``build_system_prompt`` joins these into one string; splitting them lets the prompt
    inspector show the base prompt and project context (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) as distinct
    layers without duplicating the assembly logic.
    """
    replace = _read(cwd / "SYSTEM.md") or _read(GLOBAL_DIR / "SYSTEM.md")
    append = _read(cwd / "APPEND_SYSTEM.md") or _read(GLOBAL_DIR / "APPEND_SYSTEM.md")
    base = replace if replace else BASE_SYSTEM
    if append:
        base = f"{base}\n\n{append}"
    parts: list[tuple[str, str]] = [("base", base)]
    agents = load_agents_md(cwd)
    if agents:
        parts.append(("agents.md", agents))
    return parts


def build_system_prompt(cwd: Path) -> str:
    """Base prompt + AGENTS.md context + SYSTEM.md (replace) / APPEND_SYSTEM.md (append)."""
    parts = build_system_prompt_parts(cwd)
    prompt = parts[0][1]
    for _name, text in parts[1:]:  # only "agents.md" follows base today
        prompt = f"{prompt}\n\n# Project context\n{text}"
    return prompt


def get_session_override(store) -> str:
    """Read the most recent session-level system-prompt override (a SYSTEM entry)."""
    for entry in reversed(store.entries):
        if entry.type == EntryType.SYSTEM and "override" in entry.payload:
            return entry.payload.get("override", "")
    return ""


def set_session_override(store, text: str) -> None:
    """Persist a session-level system-prompt override."""
    store.append(EntryType.SYSTEM, {"override": text})

content.text:
Found the root cause — and it's **the harness's responsibility, not yours.** Look at `BASE_SYSTEM` in `tui/context.py:10`:

> "Use the provided tools **(read, write, edit, bash, grep, find, ls)** to explore and modify the codebase."

The system prompt hard-codes the *old* seven-tool list and **never mentions `web_search`/`web_fetch`**. So the model is literally told its toolbox is the coding tools — and when asked about the web, it does the only thing it knows: `bash` + `curl`, then tries to install `lynx`/`w3m`/`html2text` to parse the gzip'd HTML garbage `curl` returned. That flailing *is* the "downloads and updates" you saw. It's the classic failure web_fetch exists to prevent — but the model was never told web_fetch exists.

## The fix: tell the model about the web tools and forbid scraping

content.text:
All 265 tests pass. Here's the full explanation and what I fixed.

## Your three questions answered

**1. Why so many downloads/updates during execution?**
Those weren't the harness — they were the **model running install commands inside `bash`**: `sudo apt-get install lynx`, `pip install html2text`, `pip install pup`, plus repeated `curl` downloads of HTML. It was trying to build itself a web browser because it didn't know it already had one. The `curl` output came back as binary gibberish (compressed HTML), so it kept escalating to heavier scrapers. Pure flailing.

**2. Harness's responsibility or the user's?**
**The harness's** — and it's now fixed. The system prompt (`tui/context.py`) literally listed the toolbox as `(read, write, edit, bash, grep, find, ls)` and never mentioned `web_search`/`web_fetch`. The model can't prefer tools it's told don't exist. I updated `BASE_SYSTEM` to:
- point the model at `web_search` → `web_fetch` for anything web/current, and
- explicitly **forbid** scraping via `curl`/`wget` or installing `lynx`/`w3m`/`html2text`.

**3. Why no real response, and the fix.**
You never got a tool-based answer because **the tools were never called** — every step was `bash`. The final "3.13.1" it produced was hallucinated from a `curl`'d directory listing (the real latest is **3.14.6**, which your tools returned correctly in our live test). Same root cause, same fix.

## Verify the fix

```bash
uv run minima -p "What's the latest stable Python release? Cite the URL."
```

You should now see it call `web_search` then `web_fetch`, and answer with **3.14.6** + a python.org URL — no `bash`, no installs.

## Two layers of defense (defense-in-depth)

| Layer | What it does | When |
|---|---|---|
| **System-prompt guidance** (just added) | Steers *every* run toward web tools, forbids scraping | Always on now |
| **Tool allowlist** `-t web_search,web_fetch` | Removes `bash` entirely so scraping is *impossible* | Use for pure web-research runs you want deterministic |

```bash
uv run minima -t web_search,web_fetch -p "latest stable Python release, cite the URL"
```

## Honest caveat
The prompt fix makes weak models *strongly prefer* the web tools, but doesn't 100% guarantee a small model (gemini-flash / haiku — which routing picked because the task looked cheap) always obeys. If you want a hard guarantee, use the `-t` allowlist; that's the only way to make scraping physically unavailable. For everyday use, the system-prompt change is the right default and should eliminate the behavior you saw.

Want me to also add a short note to your `docs/websearch-mini-projects.md` (Project 6) documenting this "system prompt must advertise the tools" lesson, and then commit everything to a branch?

file.content:
def _register_providers(cwd: Path) -> None:
    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import provider_key_present, register_catalog_models
    from minima_harness.ai.providers import ensure_providers_registered
    from minima_harness.tui.extra_models import register_extra_models

    ensure_providers_registered()
    # Register the curated multi-provider catalog, but only for providers whose key is
    # configured — so the model picker stays relevant (you see models you can actually run).
    register_catalog_models()
    # OpenRouter is an aggregator: one key unlocks its *entire* live model list (cached +
    # offline-safe), not just a few curated ids. Register it when the key is present.
    if provider_key_present("openrouter"):
        try:
            from minima_harness.ai.openrouter_catalog import register_openrouter_models

            register_openrouter_models()
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never block startup on the OpenRouter catalog
            pass
    register_extra_models(cwd)


def _overlay_minima_prices(config: HarnessConfig) -> None:
    """Best-effort: overlay Minima's authoritative live pricing onto the registered models.

    So the cost the harness reports for a call matches the cost the server routed against
    (keeps est-vs-actual honest). Offline-safe and quick: skipped without a Minima URL, short
    timeout, and any failure is swallowed (the seeded prices stand)."""
    if not (config.minima_url or "").strip():
        return
    try:
        from minima_client import MinimaClient

        from minima_harness.minima.mapping import sync_catalog

        with MinimaClient(
            config.minima_url, config.minima_api_key, timeout=min(config.timeout, 8.0)
        ) as client:
            sync_catalog(client)
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - pricing overlay must never block startup
        pass


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    _load_env_files()
    raw = sys.argv[1:] if argv is None else list(argv)

    # `minima-harness config …` — credential setup (no TUI; works before any keys exist).
    if raw and raw[0] == "config":
        from minima_harness.tui.config_cli import config_cli

        return config_cli(raw[1:])

    # `minima-harness install|list|remove …` — package management (no TUI).
    if raw and raw[0] in _PKG_COMMANDS:
        return packages_cli(raw[0], raw[1:])

    args = _build_parser().parse_args(raw)
    config = HarnessConfig.from_env()
    if args.offline:
        config.minima_url = ""
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    _register_providers(cwd)
    # Gate the routing candidate pool to models whose provider key is configured (after
    # registration so newly-added providers count) — Minima won't be offered a model the
    # user can't run. No-op when keys for the defaults (Anthropic/Gemini) are present.
    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import runnable_candidates

    config.candidates = runnable_candidates(config.candidates)
    _overlay_minima_prices(config)
    tools = _tools_for(args)

    noninteractive = args.print or args.mode in ("print", "json")
    if noninteractive:
        prompt = " ".join(args.prompt).strip()
        if not prompt:
            print("minima-harness: --print/--mode json requires a prompt", file=sys.stderr)
            return 2
        agent = MinimaAgent(
            config, tools=tools, meter=CostMeter(), system_prompt=build_system_prompt(cwd)
        )
        from minima_harness.tui.run_modes import run_json, run_print

        runner = run_json if args.mode == "json" else run_print
        return asyncio.run(runner(agent, prompt))

content.text: `--provider/--model` only affect the interactive picker — in `-p` print mode the model comes from Minima routing, so I can't force it that way. But I can **gate the candidate pool**: if Gemini's key isn't visible, routing can't pick the model that hung. Let me run the **local** binary in print mode with Gemini gated out and tools restricted to web — this is the real end-to-end verification you wanted.

content.text:
It works. The local build, run correctly, returned exactly what we wanted:

```
The latest stable Python release is **Python 3.14.6**.
Source: https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3146/
```

Correct version (matches our Exa smoke test), right URL, **no `bash`, no scraping, no Gemini hang** — it used your `web_search`/`web_fetch` tools and answered cleanly.

## What actually went wrong in your run

Two independent problems, neither in your tool code:

**1. You ran the wrong binary.** `uv run minima` executed the **Homebrew-installed `minima 0.4.10`** (`/opt/homebrew/bin/minima` → `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/...`), which is the *published* release — it has no web tools and the old system prompt. Your local edits were never loaded. This is the #1 gotcha when a tool is both `brew install`ed and developed locally: the same command name exists in two places, and PATH order decides which wins.

**2. The hang + key spam** was the *old* code's Gemini provider stalling on a streaming request, plus the google-genai SDK warning that both `GOOGLE_API_KEY` and `GEMINI_API_KEY` are set (the duplicated line). Since routing picked `gemini-2.5-flash` and that request stalled at the TLS read, it sat there until you Ctrl+C'd.

## How to always run your local build

Use the venv binary directly (unambiguous), or `uv run --`:

```bash
.venv/bin/minima -p "..."        # absolute path — always local
# or, if PATH is clean:
hash -r && uv run -- minima -p "..."
```

I verified: `.venv/bin/minima` points at your local `src/`, and the local build reports tools `[…, web_search, web_fetch]` with the new prompt. (`uv run which minima` now resolves local too — your earlier miss was likely a cached shell PATH entry for the brew binary.)

## The exact command that worked

```bash
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima -t web_search,web_fetch \
  -p "What's the latest stable Python release? Cite the URL."
```

- `.venv/bin/minima` → guarantees **local** code.
- `GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] → blanks the Google keys *for this run only*, so routing's candidate pool excludes Gemini → no stalling provider, and the duplicate-key warning disappears. (To make it permanent, `unset GOOGLE_API_KEY` in your shell profile — you don't need both; the SDK prefers `GOOGLE_API_KEY` and your `.env.harness` already has `GEMINI_API_KEY`.)
- `-t web_search,web_fetch` → removes `bash`, so scraping is impossible (the hard guarantee from last time).

For normal use you can drop `-t` now that the system prompt steers correctly — but keep the Gemini gating until that network stall is sorted.

## Open item: the Gemini stall
That hang is a network/connectivity issue reaching `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` (the request never got headers back). It's separate from your feature. If you want, I can add a streaming **timeout** to the harness's local Google provider so a stalled Gemini call fails fast and routing/feedback can react, instead of hanging until Ctrl+C. 

Want me to (a) add that Gemini stream timeout, (b) commit all the web-search work to a branch, or both?

Your minima agent (or a prior session) has modified **~65 files across the entire repo** — `src/minima`, `src/minima_harness`, tests, examples, the client SDK — and created a whole new "tips" feature (`tui/tips.py` + several `test_tip*` files). Before any next steps, I need to understand the scope, because right now your working tree mixes four unrelated things and would be impossible to review or commit cleanly.

Diagnosis confirmed: the engine.py changes are **pure `ruff format` reflow** (collapsing multi-line statements to single lines) — no logic. Your agent ran a repo-wide formatter while doing the logging fix. So your working tree is really **four unrelated piles mixed together**:

| Bucket | Files | Intent? |
|---|---|---|
| **Web-search feature** (mine + yours) | `_exa.py`, `web_search.py`, `web_fetch.py` + their tests, `builtin.py`, `tools/__init__.py`, `context.py` | ✅ intended |
| **Google logging fix** | `ai/providers/google.py` | small, probably keep |
| **A "tips" feature** | `tui/tips.py`, `app.py`, `welcome.py`, `widgets/status.py`, `test_tip*.py` | ❓ unknown — new, unrelated |
| **Repo-wide ruff reformat** | ~55 files in `src/minima`, `examples/`, `client_sdk/`, unrelated tests | ❌ noise, not your intent |

1,913 insertions / 905 deletions across 64 files — almost all of it mechanical churn you didn't ask for. **Do not commit this as-is**; it's unreviewable and buries your actual feature. Let me check the "tips" files and confirm the web tests still pass, then I'll lay out the cleanup.

=== what is tips.py? ===
from __future__ import annotations

import json

from minima_harness.tui.customize import GLOBAL_DIR

# Persisted rotation cursor: each launch / turn / `/tip` advances it so the user sees a *new* tip
# in order rather than the same one (or a random repeat). Lives next to config.env.
STATE_FILE = GLOBAL_DIR / "tips_state.json"

# Curated tips, each spotlighting one distinctive Minima command — the things a new user would
# never find on their own. Every entry leads with the `/command` so the takeaway is the command.
# Plain strings (no Textual import) so this module unit-tests in isolation.
TIPS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "/recall pulls lessons from past sessions, even across projects",
    "/escalate auto-retries on a stronger model when answer quality looks thin",
    "/confirm shows the routing trade-off panel before each turn so you pick the model",
    "/model auto lets Minima cost-route; /model <id> pins one",
    "/cache reuses a near-duplicate answer for $0 — toggle the semantic cache",
    "/optimize trims your system prompt via Mubit to save tokens",
    "/ledger set <goal> tracks spend against a budget across the whole goal",
    "/judge toggles LLM quality judging of each answer",
    "/edits forces a diff review before any file write",
    "/tree, /fork and /clone branch and revisit your session history",
    "/thoughts streams the model's reasoning live",

=== are tips files from a recent commit or brand new? ===

=== do the web-search tests still pass on current tree? ===

content.text: Ah — zsh doesn't word-split unquoted variables like bash, so the whole list became one pathspec and nothing was reverted. Let me redo it with a proper per-file loop.

count=0
for f in $(git diff --name-only); do
  case " $keep " in
    *" $f "*) ;;                       # keeper — skip
    *) git restore -- "$f" && count=$((count+1)) ;;
  esac
done
echo "reverted $count files"
echo "=== remaining tracked modifications (should be the 10 keepers) ==="
git diff --name-only

input.content:
---
name: minima-run-local-dev-build
description: How to run the LOCAL dev minima (not the Homebrew binary) and avoid the Gemini stream hang
metadata:
  type: reference
---

`minima` is both `brew install`ed (`/opt/homebrew/bin/minima`, the published release) and developed locally in this repo. `uv run minima` can silently execute the **Homebrew binary** instead of local code (PATH/shell-hash resolution), so local changes appear to do nothing.

**Always run the local build explicitly:** `.venv/bin/minima ...` (the venv script's shebang points at the repo's `src/`). Verify with `.venv/bin/python -c "import minima_harness; print(minima_harness.__file__)"` → should be under `.../minima/src/`.

**Gemini stream hang:** routing often picks `gemini-2.5-flash`; if the google-genai streaming request stalls (TLS read never returns) the run hangs until Ctrl+C, and the SDK spams "Both GOOGLE_API_KEY and GEMINI_API_KEY are set" once per init. Avoid by gating Gemini out of the candidate pool for a run: prefix `GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima ...` (blank keys → treated as absent → Anthropic/OpenAI candidates only). Permanent fix: `unset GOOGLE_API_KEY` (only one Google key is needed). See [[minima-routing-escalation-behavior]].

Note (2026-06-29): the in-repo minima TUI agent, asked to do a small change, ran a repo-wide `ruff format` and modified 64 files while reporting only 8 — isolate intended changes (`git restore` the mechanical churn) before committing.

content.text:
Done. Now your two questions.

## How to test the minima TUI

**1. Interactive TUI** (the full Textual app — what you actually want to "test"):
```bash
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima
```
- No `-p` → launches the interactive UI. The Gemini gating prefix avoids the stream hang you hit.
- Inside: type a prompt and hit Enter; try slash commands — `/help`, `/tip` (your new feature!), `/model`, `/confirm`. Exit with `Ctrl+C` or `/exit`.
- To exercise the **web tools** specifically, ask: *"latest stable Python release, cite the URL"* — you'll watch `web_search` → `web_fetch` run in the tool panel.

**2. Automated TUI tests** (headless — drive the app without a terminal, already passing):
```bash
uv run pytest tests/harness/tui -q
```
These use Textual's `Pilot` to simulate keypresses/commands. Your tips tests (`test_tip_command.py`, `test_tips.py`, `test_status_tip.py`) live here.

**3. Scriptable one-shot modes:** `-p` (print) and `--mode json` (event stream) — good for quick checks and CI.

## Next steps (prioritized)

1. **Commit the work as 3 separate, reviewable commits on a branch** — never one 64-file lump again. I'd suggest:
   - `feat(harness): Exa-backed web_search + web_fetch tools`
   - `feat(tui): /tip command with curated command hints`
   - `fix(harness): quiet google-genai logging`
2. **Project 6 is effectively done** — you already saw the real end-to-end run return `3.14.6` with a citation via the tools.
3. **Project 7** (the last guide phase): wire web-search into Minima's routing/feedback — tag search-heavy tasks so routing learns which models handle them.
4. **Optional:** add a streaming timeout to the local Google provider so a stalled Gemini call fails fast instead of hanging (the root of your earlier freeze).

Want me to **create the branch and make those three commits** now? I can stage exactly the right files for each. (I won't push or commit anything until you say go.)
On `main` (the default branch), so I'll create a feature branch first, then make the three separate commits with exactly the right files staged. `scratch/` stays out (it's your playground).

content.content:
def _build_commands(self) -> CommandRegistry:
        reg = CommandRegistry()

        async def _quit(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            app.exit()

        async def _clear(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            await app.query_one(ChatLog).remove_children()
            await app._show_welcome()

        async def _banner(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            from minima_harness.tui.welcome import render_welcome

            chatlog = app.query_one(ChatLog)
            existing = list(chatlog.query("#welcome"))
            if existing:
                for w in existing:
                    w.remove()
                chatlog.remove_class("empty")
                await chatlog.add_system("welcome hidden · /banner to show")
            else:
                w = Static(render_welcome(app), id="welcome")
                kids = list(chatlog.children)
                if kids:
                    await chatlog.mount(w, before=kids[0])
                else:
                    await chatlog.mount(w)
                    chatlog.add_class("empty")

        async def _cost(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            meter = app.agent.meter
            await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(meter.report() if meter else "(no meter)")

        async def _help(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(app.commands.help_text())

        async def _model(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            from minima_harness.ai import all_models
            from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import runnable_candidates
            from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_CANDIDATES

            def _unpin() -> None:
                # Release any pin: restore the full runnable candidate pool so Minima routes.
                app.config.candidates = runnable_candidates(list(DEFAULT_CANDIDATES))
                app.config.pinned = False
                app._footer_state["model"] = "auto"
                app._footer_state["basis"] = "minima"
                app._clear_banner()  # a prior model's error/offline banner no longer applies
                app._refresh_footer()

            # `/model auto` (or unpin/clear) releases the pin without opening the picker.
            if args.strip().lower() in ("auto", "unpin", "clear"):
                _unpin()
                await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system("model: auto — Minima routes each turn")
                return

            # Offer the union of routing candidates + every registered model (candidates first,
            # deduped) so a user can pin ANY provider's model — e.g. a Groq/DeepSeek model that
            # isn't in the default routing pool. Pinning sets candidates=[chosen].
            cands = list(app.config.candidates or [])
            cands = list(dict.fromkeys(cands + [m.id for m in all_models()]))
            providers = {m.id: m.provider for m in all_models()}
            active = app._footer_state.get("model")
            basis = app._footer_state.get("basis")
            # Pinned iff the config holds exactly one candidate (check the CONFIG, not the
            # union `cands` above which is always >1 — the old check could never detect a pin).
            pinned = app.config.candidates[0] if len(app.config.candidates or []) == 1 else None

            def _picked(chosen: str | None) -> None:
                if not chosen:
                    return
                if chosen == ModelPicker.AUTO:
                    _unpin()  # explicit "auto" entry: unpin back to Minima routing
                    return
                app.config.candidates = [chosen]  # pin → run this model directly (bypass Minima)
                app.config.pinned = True
                app._footer_state["model"] = chosen
                app._footer_state["basis"] = "pinned"
                # Clear any banner from the previous model — switching to `chosen` makes a
                # prior model's "access denied"/offline banner stale and misleading.
                app._clear_banner()
                app._refresh_footer()  # reflect the pin immediately

            app.push_screen(
                ModelPicker(
                    cands,
                    active=active,
                    basis=basis,
                    pinned=pinned,
                    providers=providers,
                ),
                callback=_picked,

            meter = app.agent.meter
            await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(meter.report() if meter else "(no meter)")

        async def _help(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(app.commands.help_text())

        async def _model(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
            from minima_harness.ai import all_models
            from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import runnable_candidates
            from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_CANDIDATES

            def _unpin() -> None:
                # Release any pin: restore the full runnable candidate pool so Minima routes.
                app.config.candidates = runnable_candidates(list(DEFAULT_CANDIDATES))
                app.config.pinned = False
                app._footer_state["model"] = "auto"
                app._footer_state["basis"] = "minima"
                app._clear_banner()  # a prior model's error/offline banner no longer applies
                app._refresh_footer()

            # `/model auto` (or unpin/clear) releases the pin without opening the picker.
            if args.strip().lower() in ("auto", "unpin", "clear"):
                _unpin()
                await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system("model: auto — Minima routes each turn")
                return

            # Offer the union of routing candidates + every registered model (candidates first,
            # deduped) so a user can pin ANY provider's model — e.g. a Groq/DeepSeek model that
            # isn't in the default routing pool. Pinning sets candidates=[chosen].
            cands = list(app.config.candidates or [])
            cands = list(dict.fromkeys(cands + [m.id for m in all_models()]))
            providers = {m.id: m.provider for m in all_models()}
            active = app._footer_state.get("model")
            basis = app._footer_state.get("basis")
            # Pinned iff the config holds exactly one candidate (check the CONFIG, not the
            # union `cands` above which is always >1 — the old check could never detect a pin).
            pinned = app.config.candidates[0] if len(app.config.candidates or []) == 1 else None

            def _picked(chosen: str | None) -> None:
                if not chosen:
                    return
                if chosen == ModelPicker.AUTO:
                    _unpin()  # explicit "auto" entry: unpin back to Minima routing
                    return
                app.config.candidates = [chosen]  # pin → run this model directly (bypass Minima)
                app.config.pinned = True
                app._footer_state["model"] = chosen
                app._footer_state["basis"] = "pinned"
                # Clear any banner from the previous model — switching to `chosen` makes a
                # prior model's "access denied"/offline banner stale and misleading.
                app._clear_banner()
                app._refresh_footer()  # reflect the pin immediately

            app.push_screen(
                ModelPicker(
                    cands,
                    active=active,
                    basis=basis,
                    pinned=pinned,
                    providers=providers,
                ),
                callback=_picked,

content.text:
Here's the authoritative command list — now I can give you an accurate hands-on guide. Everything below uses the **local** binary (`.venv/bin/minima`) and gates Gemini so it won't hang.

## 1. Launch the interactive TUI

```bash
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima
```

That opens the full Textual UI. Type a prompt, hit **Enter**. Exit anytime with `/exit`, `/quit`, or `Ctrl+C`.

> Why the prefix: blanks the Google keys for this run so routing won't pick the Gemini model that stalled on you. Drop it once your network/Gemini is sorted.

## 2. Take control of *which model runs* (most important for testing)

By default Minima routes each turn to whatever it thinks is cheapest-good — which makes tests non-deterministic. Pin a model so behavior is repeatable:

- Type **`/model`** → opens a picker → choose e.g. `claude-haiku-4-5`. Now every turn uses that model (bypasses routing).
- **`/model auto`** → release the pin, go back to Minima routing.
- Or pin from launch: routing only offers models whose key is present, so the Gemini-gating prefix above already forces Anthropic/OpenAI.

## 3. Test the web-search feature

```bash
# Force ONLY your web tools (removes bash → model must use web_search/web_fetch):
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima -t web_search,web_fetch
```
Then prompt: **"What's the latest stable Python release? Cite the URL."**
Watch the transcript: you should see a `web_search` call, then `web_fetch`, then a cited answer (3.14.6). With the full toolset (no `-t`), the new system prompt should still steer it to the web tools instead of `bash`.

## 4. Test the tips feature

- **`/tip`** → shows the next curated command hint.
- **`/tip off`** → silences the spinner tips.
- A tip also appears on launch / in the status area.

## 5. Inspection commands while testing

| Command | What it shows you |
|---|---|
| `/help` | full command list |
| `/cost` | the cost meter (est vs actual per turn) |
| `/confirm` | toggle the **routing panel before each turn** — see *why* it picked a model |
| `/thoughts` | stream the model's reasoning live |
| `/prompt` | inspect the system prompt (confirm your web-tools guidance is in there) |
| `/session`, `/tree` | session info / history branches |

`/confirm` is especially useful: turn it on and you'll see the route decision (model, cost, confidence) *before* it runs — great for understanding routing.

## 6. Tool & permission control (for safe testing)

- `-t read,grep,web_search` — allowlist only those tools.
- `-xt bash` — denylist `bash` (keep everything else).
- `-nt` / `--no-tools` — chat only, no tools.
- Inside the UI: `/edits` forces a diff review before any file write; `/yolo` toggles permission prompts (leave prompts ON while learning).

## 7. Sessions (so test runs don't pile up)

```bash
.venv/bin/minima --no-session     # ephemeral — nothing persisted
.venv/bin/minima -c               # continue the last session
.venv/bin/minima -r               # resume (pick a session)
```

## 8. Non-interactive checks (fast, scriptable)

```bash
# one-shot, prints the answer and exits:
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima -t web_search,web_fetch \
  -p "latest stable Python release, cite the URL"

# structured event stream (see every tool call as JSON):
... .venv/bin/minima --mode json -p "..."
```

`--mode json` is the best way to *see* the agent calling `web_search`/`web_fetch` step by step.

---

A typical loop for you: launch with `/model` pinned to a cheap reliable model, turn on `/confirm` and `/thoughts`, run a web-search prompt, check `/cost`, then `/tip` to exercise the tips feature. When you change harness code, just relaunch `.venv/bin/minima` — the editable install picks it up immediately (no reinstall).

Want me to add this as a short `docs/testing-the-tui.md` so it's saved alongside your other guide?

aiTitle: Build websearch capabilities for Minima harness

content.text:
Look closely at that screenshot — your tools **did** run, and the answer **is** grounded in them. The final text says `3.14.6` and cites `…/python-3146/`, which is **exactly** `web_search` result `[1]` ("Python Release Python 3.14.6 | Python.org" + that URL). The model didn't pull that from memory — it got it from the search.

What's confusing you is **display order**, not execution order. This TUI renders the final answer as the headline at the *top* of the turn block, then shows the routing badge + tool calls + cost as a "trace" *below* it. Notice the giveaway: the routing badge (`routed to…`) appears **below** the answer too — but routing happens before anything runs. So the visual order is a layout choice, not the timeline.

Let me prove the real order by running it in JSON mode, which emits events in true chronological sequence.

input.command:
cd /Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima -t web_search,web_fetch --mode json \
  -p "What's the latest stable Python release? Cite the URL." 2>/dev/null \
  | python3 -c "
import sys, json
n=0
for line in sys.stdin:
    line=line.strip()
    if not line: continue
    try: ev=json.loads(line)
    except Exception: continue
    n+=1
    t=ev.get('type') or ev.get('event') or list(ev.keys())[0]
    # summarize the interesting bits
    extra=''
    s=json.dumps(ev)
    if 'tool' in s.lower():
        extra=' '+s[:120]
    elif 'text' in ev or 'delta' in ev or 'content' in ev:
        extra=' '+s[:100]
    print(f'{n:2}. {t}{extra}')
" 2>&1 | head -50

content.text:
The background run finished — and it **settles the question completely.** Here's the true chronological event order from JSON mode:

```
Turn 1:  message → tool_start web_search   (event 11)  ✅ searched
Turn 2:  message → tool_start web_fetch    (event 23)  ✅ fetched
Turn 3:  text_delta "Based on the official Python downloads page,
                     the latest stable release is Python 3.14.6.
                     Source: …/downloads/"  (events 32–34)
         done                              (event 39)
```

The answer text is literally the **last** thing emitted (events 32–37), *after* both `web_search` (event 11) and `web_fetch` (event 23). So the model is doing exactly what you want: **search → fetch → answer.** This run even chained both tools and grounded the reply in "the official Python downloads page."

## So there's nothing to fix — it's a display artifact

In the interactive TUI, the final answer is rendered as the **headline at the top** of the turn block, with the routing badge + tool calls shown below it as a trace. That's why it *looks* like the answer came first. The dead giveaway in your screenshot: the `routed to…` badge also sits below the answer, even though routing always happens before the model runs. The layout groups "answer on top, evidence below" — it is not chronological.

## How to see the real order yourself

| Method | What you get |
|---|---|
| `--mode json -p "…"` | the raw event stream in true time order (what I ran above) |
| `/thoughts` (in TUI) | streams the model's reasoning live, so you watch it decide to search first |
| `/confirm` (in TUI) | shows the routing decision before the turn even starts |

```bash
GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED] .venv/bin/minima -t web_search,web_fetch \
  --mode json -p "What's the latest stable Python release? Cite the URL."
```

## If you want a *hard* guarantee (belt-and-suspenders)

The behavior is already correct, but to make a weak model (haiku was routed here, conf 61% "low") never even emit a preliminary guess:

1. **Pin a stronger model** in the TUI: `/model` → `claude-sonnet-4-6` (or similar). Stronger models follow "tool-first" instructions more reliably; haiku occasionally narrates before acting.
2. The system prompt already tells it to use the web tools and not answer from stale knowledge — that's the lever. I could tighten it further to *"do not state any current fact before web_search returns"* if you want, but I'd hold off: over-constraining makes the model fetch even when a search snippet already answers, which wastes time/tokens. Your earlier TUI run answered correctly from `web_search` alone — that's the model making a reasonable call.

Bottom line: your feature works correctly end-to-end. Want me to do anything else — tighten that prompt clause, pin a default model, or move on to Project 7 (routing/feedback for search-heavy tasks)?

content.text:
Here's a bullet summary of the four commits on `feat/harness-websearch`:

**`c4bcaf8` — feat(harness): Exa-backed web_search + web_fetch tools**
- Added `web_search` tool (Exa `/search`) returning ranked title + URL results
- Added `web_fetch` tool (Exa `/contents`) returning clean, readable page text with a truncation marker
- Added shared async client `_exa.py`: pydantic-validated responses, error classification (`ExaAuthError` / `ExaTransientError` / `ExaError`), and `tenacity` retries that retry **only** transient failures (429/5xx/network), never auth/4xx
- Registered both in `default_toolset()` and added a `web_toolset()` helper
- Updated the base system prompt so the model knows the web tools exist and is told **not** to scrape via `bash` or install browsers (`lynx`/`html2text`) — the root cause of the earlier flailing run
- Added the step-by-step build guide (`docs/websearch-mini-projects.md`) and `respx`-mocked unit tests covering the happy path and every error path

**`f9d101c` — feat(tui): /tip command with curated command hints**
- Added a rotating tip system (`tui/tips.py`) spotlighting hard-to-discover Minima slash-commands
- Surfaces a tip on launch and via `/tip` (`/tip off` to silence), with a persisted rotation cursor
- Wired into the app, welcome screen, and status bar; redirected the tip state file in the hermetic test fixture so tests never touch `~/.minima-harness`

**`bbe21f1` — fix(harness): quiet google-genai client logging**
- Set the `google` logger to `WARNING` when building the Gemini client, eliminating the verbose per-init spam (including the duplicate "both GOOGLE_API_KEY and GEMINI_API_KEY are set" notice)

**`ff797ef` — chore: gitignore local scratch/ playground**
- Excluded the experimental throwaway files used while building the feature from version control

**Verification across the work:** full harness suite **478 passed / 4 skipped**, `ruff` clean, and confirmed live end-to-end (search → fetch → answer returning the correct Python 3.14.6 with citation).

Want these condensed into a PR description?

Based on the search results, here's information about configuring the Exa API key for OpenCode web search:

## Configuration Methods

### 1. **Environment Variable Method**
You need to set the EXA_API_KEY environment variable. On macOS/Linux, use `export EXA_API_KEY=[REDACTED]`, and on Windows use `setx EXA_API_KEY "your-api-key-here"`.

You can check if the variable is set with `echo $EXA_API_KEY`, and to add it permanently to your shell config, use `echo 'export EXA_API_KEY=[REDACTED]' >> ~/.zshrc`.

### 2. **OpenCode Configuration File Method**
In your OpenCode configuration, you can specify the Exa MCP URL with the API key: `"url": "https://mcp.exa.ai/mcp?tools=web_search_exa,get_code_context_exa,crawling_exa&exaApiKey={env:EXA_API_KEY}"`.

### 3. **Alternative MCP Configuration**
To overcome free plan rate limits and enable production use, add your own API key with headers: `"headers": { "x-api-key": "YOUR_EXA_API_KEY" }`.

## Getting Your API Key

To get your Exa API key: Visit Exa AI, sign up for an account, create an API Key in Dashboard, and add the key to environment variables.

## Important Notes

- OpenCode's Exa support is disabled by default, though the OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA flag enables web search with limited (but generous) rate limits.
- Exa MCP has a generous free plan.

Links: [{"title":"Tools | OpenCode","url":"https://opencode.ai/docs/tools/"},{"title":"Web Search & Fetch: Let AI Access Latest Information | OpenCode Tutorial - AI 编程助手实战指南","url":"https://learnopencode.com/en/5-advanced/23-web-search"},{"title":"Built-in Tools Reference - OpenCode Docs","url":"https://open-code.ai/en/docs/tools"},{"title":"Basehub","url":"https://forums.basehub.com/anomalyco/opencode/34"},{"title":"Getting more out of OpenCode","url":"https://ricostacruz.com/posts/opencode-starter-kit"},{"title":"Web Search MCP - Exa","url":"https://exa.ai/docs/reference/exa-mcp"},{"title":"[FEATURE]: Use provider-hosted web search when available · Issue #10704 · anomalyco/opencode","url":"https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/10704"},{"title":"@joshthomas/opencode-web-search - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/@joshthomas/opencode-web-search"}]

Based on the search results, here's how OpenCode's webfetch web search tool works with Exa:

## How It Works

OpenCode performs web searches using Exa AI to find relevant information online, useful for researching topics, finding current events, or gathering information beyond the training data cutoff.

Use websearch when you need to find information (discovery), and webfetch when you need to retrieve content from a specific URL (retrieval). webfetch fetches raw HTML/text and converts it to Markdown using turndown.

## Technical Implementation

The tool works by sending a JSON-RPC request to Exa's hosted MCP endpoint with parameters like query, numResults, livecrawl (cached vs. live), and type (auto, fast, deep). The tool processes the response as a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream, specifically looking for data lines that contain the search results in Markdown format.

## Authentication

No API key is required — the tool connects directly to Exa AI's hosted MCP service without authentication. However, this tool is only available when using the OpenCode provider or when the OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA environment variable is set to any truthy value.

## Limitations

webfetch has a 5MB size limit, and if the target page is too large (like a huge PDF), it will error with Response too large.

lastPrompt: I provided Exa api key in my harness env, how can I provide Exa api key for the production? How does OpenCode provides it?

aiTitle: Build websearch capabilities for Minima harness

content.text:
Here's the full picture — and they differ in an important architectural way.

## How OpenCode does it (verified)

OpenCode doesn't call Exa's REST API directly like you do — it connects to **Exa's hosted MCP server** (`mcp.exa.ai/mcp`):

- **By default, no API key at all.** It hits Exa's hosted MCP unauthenticated, relying on Exa's free/generous rate limits. It's gated behind the `OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA` env flag (or auto-on when using the OpenCode provider).
- **For production / beyond the free tier, you supply your own key** in the MCP server config, sourced from an env var — either as a URL query param `?exaApiKey={env:EXA_API_KEY}` or as a header `"x-api-key": "..."`.

So OpenCode's "production key" answer is: **set `EXA_API_KEY` in the environment and reference it from the MCP config block.** The key is always env-sourced; the MCP layer is just a proxy.

**Key difference from your harness:** you call `api.exa.ai/search` + `/contents` directly with `x-api-key`, reading `os.environ["EXA_API_KEY"]`. So you don't copy OpenCode's MCP URL — but the *principle* is identical: the key lives in the environment, never in code.

## How to provide it in production for *your* harness

Your harness is a CLI shipped via Homebrew/PyPI and run on end-user machines, so "production" = the installed `minima` on someone's machine (no `.env.harness` in cwd). Your codebase already has the right mechanism — the **per-user config store** (`tui/config_store.py`): keyring-first, `~/.minima-harness/config.env` (0600) fallback, hydrated into `os.environ` at startup. That's how `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MUBIT_API_KEY`, etc. are stored via `minima config set`.

Your precedence ladder (already built):
1. Shell env / `--env-file` (highest) — what servers/CI use
2. Project `.env.harness` / `.env` — what **you** did in dev ✅
3. Per-user config store (keyring / 0600 file) — the production path for end users

**The gap:** `EXA_API_KEY` isn't registered in the config store's `SECTIONS`, so `minima config set EXA_API_KEY …` isn't first-class — production users can currently only set it via raw env/`.env`. Since `_exa.py` reads `os.environ.get("EXA_API_KEY")`, it *works* once it's in the env, but it's not discoverable via `minima config`.

So the production recommendation, by deployment shape:

| Deployment | How to supply the key |
|---|---|
| **Distributed CLI** (most likely) | Add `EXA_API_KEY` to `config_store.SECTIONS` → users run `minima config set EXA_API_KEY …` → stored in OS keyring, masked in `config list`. **(small code change — see below)** |
| **Server / container / CI** | Inject `EXA_API_KEY` as an env var from a secret manager (AWS/GCP Secret Manager, k8s Secret, Doppler, Vault). Already works — no code change. **Never bake the key into the image.** |
| **Your dev box** | `.env.harness` (what you're doing) — fine for dev, gitignored. |

## The one change worth making

Register Exa in the config store so it's a first-class, keyring-backed credential like the others — e.g. a new section in `tui/config_store.py`:

```python
Section(
    title="Web tools",
    note="Exa API key for web_search / web_fetch (https://exa.ai).",
    fields=(Field("EXA_API_KEY", "Exa API key (web search)", optional=True),),
),
```

Then `minima config set EXA_API_KEY exa_…` stores it in the keyring, `hydrate_env()` puts it in `os.environ` at launch, and `_exa.py` picks it up — works from any directory, no `.env` needed. I'd also add it to `config doctor`'s checklist so users can verify it.

Want me to implement that (config-store field + `config doctor` check + a test) as a follow-up commit on the branch?

Sources:
- [Tools | OpenCode](https://opencode.ai/docs/tools/)
- [Web Search & Fetch tutorial — learnopencode](https://learnopencode.com/en/5-advanced/23-web-search)
- [Is it possible to increase Exa websearch by including an API Key? — opencode#15953](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/15953)
- [Web Search MCP — Exa docs](https://exa.ai/docs/reference/exa-mcp)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Per-user credential store for the harness — keyring-first, 0600-file fallback.

Mirrors how best-in-class coding-agent CLIs persist secrets (Claude Code: macOS Keychain,
else ``~/.<tool>/...`` at mode 0600). Secrets go to the OS keyring when a real backend is
available; otherwise they fall back to ``~/.minima-harness/config.env`` written 0600.
Non-secret config (URLs) always lives in the file — no point keychaining a URL.

The harness itself reads everything from environment variables (provider keys via
``resolve_api_key``, Mubit via ``os.environ``), so :func:`hydrate_env` materialises stored
values into ``os.environ`` at startup with ``setdefault`` — keeping the store the *lowest*
precedence (real shell env and project ``.env`` files still win).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL
from minima_harness.tui.customize import GLOBAL_DIR

CONFIG_FILE = GLOBAL_DIR / "config.env"
KEYRING_SERVICE = "minima-harness"


def _keyring():  # noqa: ANN202 - the keyring module type is optional/dynamic
    """Return the keyring module iff a *real* (non-fail) backend is available, else None."""
    try:
        import keyring
        from keyring.backends import fail

        if isinstance(keyring.get_keyring(), fail.Keyring):
            return None
        return keyring
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - keyring is optional; any failure → file fallback
        return None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Field:
    """One configurable value. ``secret`` fields are masked + keyring-eligible."""

    key: str
    label: str
    secret: bool = True
    optional: bool = False
    default: str = ""
    aliases: tuple[str, ...] = ()


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Section:
    title: str
    note: str
    fields: tuple[Field, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)


def _provider_fields() -> tuple[Field, ...]:
    """Build the LLM-provider key fields from the provider catalog (single source of truth)."""
    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import config_providers

    fields: list[Field] = []
    for p in config_providers():
        primary, *alts = p.env_vars
        label = f"{p.display_name} — {p.blurb}" if p.blurb else f"{p.display_name} API key"
        fields.append(Field(primary, label, optional=True, aliases=tuple(alts)))
    return tuple(fields)


SECTIONS: tuple[Section, ...] = (
    Section(
        title="LLM provider keys",
        note="Keys to RUN the chosen model — set any one (or several). More providers "
        "(Fireworks, DeepInfra, Cerebras, Perplexity, Cohere, …) work by exporting their env "
        "var; local runtimes (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio) need no key.",
        fields=_provider_fields(),
    ),
    Section(
        title="Mubit / Minima routing",
        note="Mubit memory backend + the Minima recommender endpoint.",
        fields=(
            Field("MUBIT_API_KEY", "Mubit API key (memory + routing auth)"),
            Field(
                "MINIMA_URL",
                "Minima endpoint URL",
                secret=False,
                optional=True,
                default=DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL,
            ),
            Field(
                "MINIMA_API_KEY",
                "Minima auth (optional; falls back to MUBIT_API_KEY)",
                optional=True,
            ),
            Field("MUBIT_ENDPOINT", "Mubit endpoint URL", secret=False, optional=True),
        ),
    ),
)


def all_fields() -> list[Field]:
    return [f for section in SECTIONS for f in section.fields]


def field_for(key: str) -> Field | None:
    return next((f for f in all_fields() if f.key == key), None)


def backend_name() -> str:
    """The active secrets backend label, for display."""
    return "keyring" if _keyring() is not None else "file"


def mask(value: str | None) -> str:
    """Show only the last 4 chars of a secret (never the whole thing)."""
    if not value:
        return ""
    if len(value) <= 4:
        return "•" * len(value)
    return "•" * 4 + value[-4:]


# --- file backend (env-format, mode 0600) ---------------------------------------------


def _read_file() -> dict[str, str]:
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    if not CONFIG_FILE.is_file():
        return out
    for raw in CONFIG_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
        line = raw.strip()
        if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
            continue
        k, _, v = line.partition("=")
        out[k.strip()] = v.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
    return out


def _write_file(data: dict[str, str]) -> None:
    GLOBAL_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    lines = [f"{k}={v}" for k, v in sorted(data.items()) if v != ""]
    body = "\n".join(["# minima-harness config — managed by `minima-harness config`", *lines])
    # O_CREAT with 0600 so the file is owner-only from the moment it exists.
    fd = os.open(CONFIG_FILE, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o600)
    try:
        os.write(fd, (body + "\n").encode("utf-8"))
    finally:
        os.close(fd)
    try:  # tighten perms even if the file pre-existed with looser ones
        os.chmod(CONFIG_FILE, 0o600)
    except OSError:
        pass


def _file_set(key: str, value: str) -> None:
    data = _read_file()
    data[key] = value
    _write_file(data)


def _file_delete(key: str) -> None:
    data = _read_file()
    if key in data:
        del data[key]
        _write_file(data)


# --- public get / set / unset ----------------------------------------------------------


def get(key: str) -> str | None:
    """Read a stored value: keyring first for secrets, then the file."""
    f = field_for(key)
    secret = [REDACTED] if f else True
    if secret:
        kr = _keyring()
        if kr is not None:
            try:
                val = kr.get_password(KEYRING_SERVICE, key)
                if val:
                    return val
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                pass
    return _read_file().get(key)


def set_value(key: str, value: str) -> str:
    """Persist ``value``. Returns the backend used: ``"keyring"`` or ``"file"``."""
    f = field_for(key)
    secret = [REDACTED] if f else True
    if secret:
        kr = _keyring()
        if kr is not None:
            try:
                kr.set_password(KEYRING_SERVICE, key, value)
                _file_delete(key)  # don't leave a stale plaintext copy behind
                return "keyring"
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                pass
    _file_set(key, value)
    return "file"


def unset(key: str) -> None:
    kr = _keyring()
    if kr is not None:
        try:
            kr.delete_password(KEYRING_SERVICE, key)
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
            pass
    _file_delete(key)


def location(key: str) -> str:
    """Where ``key`` is stored: ``"keyring"`` | ``"file"`` | ``"—"`` (unset)."""
    f = field_for(key)
    secret = [REDACTED] if f else True
    if secret:
        kr = _keyring()
        if kr is not None:
            try:
                if kr.get_password(KEYRING_SERVICE, key):
                    return "keyring"
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                pass
    return "file" if key in _read_file() else "—"


def hydrate_env() -> None:
    """Load stored config into ``os.environ`` (setdefault → real env / project files win)."""
    for f in all_fields():
        val = get(f.key)
        if not val:
            continue
        os.environ.setdefault(f.key, val)
        for alias in f.aliases:
            os.environ.setdefault(alias, val)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""``minima config`` — the pre-TUI credential setup command.

Sectioned guided setup plus non-interactive ``list``/``get``/``set``/``unset``/``doctor``/
``path``. Secrets are never echoed: interactive entry uses ``getpass``, and ``list``/``get``
mask values to the last 4 characters. Backed by :mod:`minima_harness.tui.config_store`.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import getpass
import os
import sys

from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL
from minima_harness.tui import config_store as store

_USAGE = (
    "usage: minima config "
    "[list | get <KEY> | set <KEY> <VALUE> | unset <KEY> | doctor | path]\n"
    "       minima config            # interactive guided setup"
)


def _list() -> int:
    print(f"config file:     {store.CONFIG_FILE}")
    print(f"secrets backend: {store.backend_name()} (service '{store.KEYRING_SERVICE}')\n")
    for section in store.SECTIONS:
        print(f"[{section.title}]")
        for f in section.fields:
            val = store.get(f.key)
            if val:
                shown = store.mask(val) if f.secret else val
                print(f"  {f.key:<20} {shown:<26} ({store.location(f.key)})")
            else:
                print(f"  {f.key:<20} {'—':<26} ({'optional' if f.optional else 'MISSING'})")
        print()
    return 0


def _interactive() -> int:
    print("minima config — press Enter to keep the current value.\n")
    for section in store.SECTIONS:
        print(f"# {section.title} — {section.note}")
        for f in section.fields:
            cur = store.get(f.key)
            if f.secret:
                shown = store.mask(cur) if cur else "unset"
                entered = getpass.getpass(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            else:
                shown = cur or f.default or "unset"
                entered = input(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            if entered:
                print(f"    saved → {store.set_value(f.key, entered)}")
            elif not cur and f.default and not f.secret:
                store.set_value(f.key, f.default)
                print(f"    saved default → {f.default}")
        print()
    print("done. Run `minima config doctor` to verify.")
    return 0


def _doctor() -> int:
    store.hydrate_env()
    print("config doctor\n")
    providers = [
        ("Anthropic", "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"),
        ("Gemini", "GEMINI_API_KEY"),
        ("OpenAI", "OPENAI_API_KEY"),
        ("Mubit", "MUBIT_API_KEY"),
    ]
    for label, key in providers:
        ok = bool(os.environ.get(key))
        print(f"  [{'ok' if ok else '  '}] {label:<10} {key:<18} {'present' if ok else 'missing'}")

    url = os.environ.get("MINIMA_URL", DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL)
    print(f"\n  Minima endpoint: {url}")
    import httpx

    reachable = False
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/health", timeout=5.0)
        print(f"  health: HTTP {resp.status_code}")
        reachable = True
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any failure is just 'unreachable'
        print(f"  health: unreachable ({type(exc).__name__})")

    # Authenticated probe: /v1/health is unauthenticated, so it can't tell a valid key from a
    # bad one. A minimal recommend with the key actually exercises the routing auth path.
    key = os.environ.get("MINIMA_API_KEY") or os.environ.get("MUBIT_API_KEY")
    if not reachable:
        return 0
    if not key:
        print("  auth: no MUBIT_API_KEY/MINIMA_API_KEY set — routing falls back to offline")
        return 0
    try:
        r = httpx.post(
            url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/recommend",
            headers={"authorization": f"Bearer {key}"},
            json={"task": {"task": "minima config doctor probe"}, "cost_quality_tradeoff": 5},
            timeout=15.0,
        )
        if r.status_code == 200:
            print("  auth: OK — key accepted")
        elif r.status_code in (401, 403):
            print(f"  auth: FAILED — key rejected (HTTP {r.status_code})")
        else:
            print(f"  auth: HTTP {r.status_code}")
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        print(f"  auth: probe failed ({type(exc).__name__})")
    return 0


def config_cli(args: list[str]) -> int:
    if not args:
        return _interactive()
    cmd, rest = args[0], args[1:]
    if cmd == "list":
        return _list()
    if cmd == "path":
        print(store.CONFIG_FILE)
        print(f"secrets backend: {store.backend_name()} (service '{store.KEYRING_SERVICE}')")
        return 0
    if cmd == "doctor":
        return _doctor()
    if cmd == "get" and rest:
        val = store.get(rest[0])
        if val is None:
            print(f"{rest[0]}: unset", file=sys.stderr)
            return 1
        f = store.field_for(rest[0])
        print(store.mask(val) if (f is None or f.secret) else val)  # secrets stay masked
        return 0
    if cmd == "set" and len(rest) >= 2:
        print(f"set {rest[0]} → {store.set_value(rest[0], rest[1])}")
        return 0
    if cmd == "unset" and rest:
        store.unset(rest[0])
        print(f"unset {rest[0]}")
        return 0
    print(_USAGE, file=sys.stderr)
    return 2
